text: <sup>{The Book of the Prophet}</sup> <sup>{Isaiah}</sup> d <sup>{The Vision of Isaiah}</sup> d This <sup>{This book has been translated from the Great Isaiah Scroll (1QIsa a ) of the DSS. The MT, LXX, Syr, Targ, and other ancient texts are compared in footnotes where they may vary from 1QIsa a and other DSS mss. Some of these ancient readings were incorporated instead of the DSS Isaiah text.}</sup> is the vision that Amoz's son Isaiah <sup>{The Heb. name Isaiah means The Lord has saved}</sup> had about Judah and Jerusalem during the reigns <sup>{Lit. days}</sup> of Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, kings of Judah.

<sup>{Rebellious Judah}</sup> d Listen, you heavens, and let the <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks the}</sup> earth pay attention, because the Lord has spoken: "I reared children and brought them to adulthood, but then they rebelled against me.

The ox knows its owner, and the donkey its master's feeding trough, but <sup>{So 4QIsa j ; 1QIsa a MT lack but}</sup> Israel doesn't know, and <sup>{So 4QIsa j ; 1QIsa a MT lack and}</sup> my people don't understand.

"Oh, you sinful nation! You people burdened down by iniquity! You offspring of those who keep practicing what is evil! You corrupt children! "They've abandoned the Lord ; they've despised the Holy One of Israel; in their estrangement, they've walked away from me. <sup>{Lit. they've gone back}</sup>
"Why will you still be struck down? Why will you continue to rebel? Your whole head is sick, and your whole heart is faint.

From the sole of your foot to the top of your head, there's no soundness evident <sup>{Lit. soundness in it}</sup> only bruises, sores, and festering wounds that haven't been cleaned out, bandaged, or treated <sup>{Or softened}</sup> with oil."

<sup>{God's Diagnosis}</sup> d "Your country lies desolate; your cities have been incinerated. Before your very eyes, foreigners are devouring your land they've brought devastation on it, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MurIsa MT LXX read Devastated}</sup> while the land is <sup>{DSS MT lack the land is}</sup> overthrown by foreigners.
"The daughter of Zion is left abandoned, like a booth in a vineyard, like a hut in a cucumber field, or like a city under siege.
If the Lord of the Heavenly Armies hadn't left us a few survivors, we would be like Sodom; we would be like Gomorrah.
"Listen to what the Lord says, you rulers of Sodom, and <sup>{So 1:10 1QIsa a Syr; MT LXX Targ Vulgate lack and}</sup> pay attention to the teaching of our God, you people of Gomorrah!
"How do your voluminous sacrifices benefit me?" the Lord is asking. "I've had enough of burnt offerings of rams and the fat of well-fed beasts. I don't enjoy the blood of bulls, lambs, or goats.
"When you come to present yourselves in my presence, <sup>{Lit. come for my face to appear}</sup> who has required you to trample on my courts?
Stop bringing useless offerings! Incense is detestable to me, as are your New Moons, Sabbaths, and calling of convocations. I cannot stand iniquity within <sup>{Lit. and}</sup> a solemn assembly.

As for your New Moons and your appointed festivals, I abhor <sup>{Lit. festivals, my soul abhors}</sup> them. They've become a burden to me; I've grown weary of carrying that burden. <sup>{Lit. carrying them}</sup>

When you spread out your hands in prayer, <sup>{DSS MT lack in prayer}</sup> I'll hide my eyes from you. Even though you pray repeatedly, I won't listen. Your hands are full of blood, your fingers drenched <sup>{DSS lack drenched}</sup> with iniquity." <sup>{MT 4QIsa f lack this line}</sup>

<sup>{An Invitation to Reconciliation}</sup> d "Wash yourselves, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa f MT lack and}</sup> make yourselves clean; remove your evil behavior from my presence; stop practicing what is evil.

Learn to practice what is good; seek justice, alleviate oppression, <sup>{Or rescue the oppressed}</sup> defend orphans <sup>{Or defend the fatherless}</sup> in court, and <sup>{DSS MT lack and}</sup> plead the widow's case.

"Please come, and let's reason together,"implores the Lord . "Even though your <sup>{Lit. your (pl.)}</sup> sins are like scarlet, they'll be white like snow. Though they're like crimson, <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; 4QIsa f MT read they're red like crimson}</sup> they'll become like wool.
If you're willing and obedient, you'll eat the best that the land produces;

but <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX Targ Syr; 4QIsa f lacks but}</sup> if you refuse and rebel, you'll be devoured by the sword, <sup>{So 1QIsa a Targ Syr; LXX reads the sword will devour you}</sup> because the Lord <sup>{Lit. Lord's mouth}</sup> has spoken."
<sup>{Diagnosis and Judgment}</sup> d "How the faithful city has become a whore, she who used to be filled with justice! Righteousness used to reside within her, but now only murderers live there.
Your silver has <sup>{So MT; 1QIsa a reads have}</sup> become dross, your best wine is diluted with water.

Your princes are rebels and companions of thieves. All of them are lovers of <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads Everyone loves}</sup> bribes and are runners <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads and run}</sup> after gifts. They don't bring justice to orphans, <sup>{Or to the fatherless}</sup> and the widow's case never comes up for review in their court." <sup>{Lit. comes before them}</sup>

<sup>{Restoration and Redemption}</sup> d Therefore this is what the Lord God of the Heavenly Armies, the one who is Israel's Mighty One, declares: "Now I'll get relief from his <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa f MT LXX Vulgate read my}</sup> enemies and avenge myself on his <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; LXX MT read my}</sup> foes.

When I turn <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; LXX MT read And let me turn}</sup> my attention to you, <sup>{Lit. my hand against you}</sup> I'll refine your dross as in a furnace. <sup>{1QIsa a lacks as in a furnace ; MT reads as lye}</sup> I'll remove <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa f ; MT reads Let me remove}</sup> all your alloy.

Let me restore <sup>{1QIsa a MT; 4QIsa f reads I will restore}</sup> your judges as at the first, and your counselors as at the beginning. Afterward you'll be called «The Righteous City'and «The Faithful City of Zion'. <sup>{So LXX; the Heb. lacks Zion}</sup>
"Zion <sup>{So MT; 1QIsa a reading unclear}</sup> will be redeemed by justice, and her repentant ones <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT 4QIsa f ; LXX reads her captivity}</sup> by righteousness.
Rebels and sinners will be broken together, and those who forsake the Lord will be consumed.
They'll be ashamed of the oak trees that you desired; and you'll blush because of the gardens that you have chosen.
You'll be like an oak whose leaf is withering, like an unwatered garden.

Your <sup>{Lit. Your (pl.); so 1QIsa a Vulgate. The}</sup> strong one <sup>{LXX MT read Their strength}</sup> will be like tinder, and your work <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads their work ; LXX reads their deeds}</sup> a spark; both of them will burn together, with no one to quench the flames that burn <sup>{DSS MT lack the flames that burn}</sup> them."
<sup>{A Message for Judah and Jerusalem}</sup> d The message that Amoz's son Isaiah received <sup>{Lit. observed}</sup> concerning Judah and Jerusalem:

"It will come about in the last days that the mountain that is the Lord's Temple will be established as the highest of mountains, <sup>{So 4QIsa e MT LXX; 1QIsa a lacks of mountains}</sup> and will be raised above the hills; all the nations will stream to <sup>{Or will flow over ; so 1QIsa a LXX; 4QIsa e 4QIsa f MT LXX read will travel to ; cf. Mic 4:1}</sup> it.

Many groups of people <sup>{Lit. Many peoples}</sup> will come, commenting, "Come! Let's go up to <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa f ; 4QIsa e MT read to the Lord's mountain, to}</sup> the Temple of the God of Jacob, that they <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; cf. LXX, Mic 4:2; 4QIsa e MT LXX read he}</sup> may teach us his ways. Then let's walk in his paths." <sup>{The Rule of God from Jerusalem}</sup> d"Instruction <sup>{Or For the law}</sup> will proceed from Zion, and the word of the Lord from Jerusalem.

He will judge between the nations, and will render verdicts <sup>{Or judgment}</sup> for the benefit of many. <sup>{Lit. many peoples}</sup> "They will beat their swords into plowshares, and their spears into pruning hooks; nations will not raise swords against nations, and they will not learn warfare anymore.
"You house of Jacob! Come! Let's live <sup>{Lit. walk}</sup> in the Lord's light.

For you have rejected your people, the house of Jacob, because they are filled with practices learned <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack with practices learned}</sup> from the East and they are fortune-tellers like the Philistines. They cut deals <sup>{Lit. They shake hands}</sup> with foreigners. <sup>{Lit. with descendants of foreigners}</sup>
Their land is filled with silver and gold, and there is no end to their treasures; their land is filled with horses, and there is no end to their chariots.
Their land is filled with idols; they bow down to the work of their hands, to what their own fingers have made.
"So mankind is humbled, each human being is brought low, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa b ; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> you won't forgive." <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa b ; MT reads forgive them}</sup>

<sup>{The Coming Day of the Lord}</sup> d <sup>{This v. is missing from 1 QIsa a}</sup> "Go into the rocks! Hide in the dust to escape <sup>{Lit. from}</sup> the terror of the Lord and to escape <sup>{Lit. and from}</sup> the glory of his majesty! <sup>{So MT}</sup>

The <sup>{Lit. And the}</sup> haughty looks of mankind will be brought low, <sup>{1QIsa a MT LXX read mankind are low}</sup> the lofty pride of human beings will be humbled, and the Lord alone will be exalted at that time. <sup>{Lit. in that day}</sup>

"For the Lord of the Heavenly Armies has reserved <sup>{1QIsa a MT LXX lack reserved}</sup> a time <sup>{Lit. day}</sup> to oppose <sup>{Lit. against}</sup> all who are proud and haughty, and the <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read oppose all of the}</sup> self-exalting they will be humbled.
He will take his stand <sup>{DSS MT LXX lack He will take his stand}</sup> against all the cedars of Lebanon, against the proud and self-exalting; and against all the oaks of Bashan;
against all the high mountains, and against all the lofty hills;
against every high tower, and against every fortified wall;
against all the ships from Tarshish, and against all their impressive watercraft.
"Humanity's haughtiness will be humbled, male arrogance will be brought low, and the Lord alone will be exalted in that day.
Their <sup>{DSS MT LXX lack Their}</sup> idols will utterly vanish. <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads He will abolish the idols}</sup>

"They will enter caverns in the rocks and holes in the ground to escape <sup>{Lit. from}</sup> the presence of the terror of the Lord , to escape <sup>{Lit. from}</sup> the splendor of his majesty when he arises to terrify the earth.

At that time, <sup>{Lit. day}</sup> mankind will throw their silver and gold idols <sup>{Lit. silver idols and gold idols}</sup> that their fingers have made <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads that they made for themselves ; LXX reads that they made}</sup> as objects of worship to the moles and to the bats.

They will enter caverns in the rocks and clefts in the cliffs, to escape <sup>{Lit. from}</sup> the terror of the Lord and to escape <sup>{Lit. from}</sup> the splendor of his majesty, when he arises to terrorize the earth.
"Stop trusting in human beings, whose life breath is in their nostrils, for what are they <sup>{Lit. what is he}</sup> really worth?" <sup>{LXX lacks this verse}</sup>

<sup>{Judgment Comes to Judah's Leaders}</sup> d "Note this! The Lord G od of the Heavenly Armies is taking away from Jerusalem and Judah everything that your society needs <sup>{Lit. Judah both supply and support}</sup> all food supplies and all water supplies,
the mighty man and the warrior, the judge and the prophet, the fortune-teller and the elder,
the commander of fifty and the man of rank, and the counselor, the expert magician, and the medium.
"I will make youths their princes, and infants will rule over them.
People will oppress one another It will be man against man and neighbor against neighbor. The young will be disrespectful to the old, and the worthless to the honorable.
"For a man will grab his brother in his own father's house, and say, «You have a cloak, so you be our leader, and this heap of ruins will be under your rule!'

"But <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; the Heb. lacks But}</sup> at that time, <sup>{Lit. day}</sup> he'll protest! <sup>{Lit. he'll cry out}</sup> He'll say, «I won't be your healer. I have neither food nor clothing in my house! You're not going to make me a leader of the people!'

"For Jerusalem has stumbled, and Judah has fallen, because what they say and do opposes <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read do is towards}</sup> the Lord; they keep defying him. <sup>{Lit. defying his glorious presence}</sup>

"The expressions on their faces give them away. <sup>{Lit. faces bears witness against them}</sup> They parade their sin around like Sodom; they don't even try to <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack try to}</sup> hide it. How horrible it will be for them, because they have brought disaster on themselves!"

<sup>{Encouragement to the Righteous}</sup> d "Tell <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks Tell}</sup> the righteous that things will go well, because they will enjoy <sup>{Lit. eat}</sup> the fruit of their actions."

<sup>{Warning to the Wicked}</sup> d "How terrible it will be for the wicked! Disaster is headed their way, because what they did with their hand <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read hands}</sup> will be repaid <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads done}</sup> to them.

"As for my people, children <sup>{Or youths}</sup> are their oppressors, and women rule over them. My people, your leaders are misleading you they're giving you confusing directions." <sup>{So MT; 1QIsa a reads they're devouring your paths}</sup>
<sup>{When God Goes to Court}</sup> d The Lord is taking his place to argue his case; he's standing up to judge the people.

The Lord will go to court <sup>{Lit. go into judgment}</sup> to oppose <sup>{Lit. with}</sup> the elders and princes of his people: "You're the ones who have been devouring the vineyard, the plunder of the poor is in your own houses!

How dare you crush <sup>{Lit. What do you mean by crushing}</sup> my people as you grind down the face of the poor?" declares the Lord God of the Heavenly Armies. <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; LXX lacks this line}</sup>

<sup>{Judgment of Jerusalem's Women}</sup> d The Lord also says: "Because Zion's women are so haughty, and walk with outstretched necks, flirting with their eyes, prancing <sup>{Or mincing}</sup> along as they walk, and making tinkling noises with their ankle bracelets, <sup>{Lit. their feet}</sup>

therefore the Lord <sup>{So 1QIsa a corrector; 1QIsa a 4QIsa b MT read my Lord ; LXX reads God}</sup> will afflict sores on the heads of Zion's women, and the Lord <sup>{So 1QIsa a corrector MT; 1QIsa a reads my Lord}</sup> will expose their private parts.
"At that time, <sup>{Lit. In that day}</sup> the Lord <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT 1QIsa a corrector read my Lord}</sup> will take away the finery of the ankle bracelets, headbands, crescents,
pendants, bracelets, veils,
headdresses, armlets, sashes, perfume boxes, charms,
signet rings, nose rings,
fine robes, capes, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa b MT read capes and cloaks}</sup> purses,
mirrors, linen garments, tiaras, and veils.

"And it will come about that instead of fragrance there will be <sup>{The 1QIsa a lacks will be a stench}</sup> a stench; instead of a belt, a rope; instead of well-set hair, baldness; instead of a fine robe, sackcloth; and instead of beauty, shame. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads burning instead of beauty ; LXX lacks this line}</sup>
Your men will die violently, <sup>{Lit. will fall by the sword}</sup> while your forces <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads force}</sup> fall <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack fall}</sup> in battle
and her gates lament and mourn. Ravaged, she will sit on the ground."

"At that time, <sup>{Lit. day}</sup> seven women will cling tightly to one man and will make him this offer: <sup>{Lit. will say}</sup> «We'll provide <sup>{Lit. eat}</sup> our own bread. We'll provide our own clothes. Just let us marry you <sup>{Lit. let your name be upon us}</sup> so we won't be stigmatized anymore.»" <sup>{I.e. by appearing to be part of a family}</sup>

<sup>{The Future Glory of Jerusalem}</sup> d "At that time, <sup>{Lit. In that day}</sup> the Lord's branch will be beautiful and glorious, and the fruit of the land will be the pride and glory of the survivors of Israel and Judah. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack and Judah}</sup>
Whoever <sup>{Lit. It will come about that whoever}</sup> survives in Zion and whoever remains in Jerusalem will be called holy everyone who has been appointed to survive in Jerusalem

when the Lord will have washed away the filth of the women <sup>{Lit. daughters}</sup> of Zion, cleaning up Jerusalem's guilt <sup>{Lit. blood ; i.e. guilt incurred by shedding innocent blood}</sup> by a spirit of judgment and a spirit of tempest. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads of burning}</sup>

Then the Lord will create over the entire site of Mount Zion including over those who assemble there a cloud by day <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX reads day, accompanied by smoke, as well as the brilliance of a flaming fire by night, because over the entire glorious area there will be a canopy and 6 a shelter to protect from the heat of the day, and}</sup>
and also to serve as a refuge and shelter from storms and rain."

<sup>{The Lord's Vineyard}</sup> d I will sing <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads Please, let me sing}</sup> for my beloved my love-song concerning his vineyard: "The one I love had a vineyard on a very fertile hill.

He plowed its land <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack its land}</sup> and cleared it of stones. Then he planted it with the choicest vines, built a watchtower in the middle of it, and dug a wine vat in it; He expected <sup>{Or waited for}</sup> it to produce good <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack good}</sup> grapes, but it produced only wild ones." <sup>{I.e. grapes unsuitable for wine making}</sup>
"So now, you inhabitants <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads inhabitant}</sup> of Jerusalem and men of Judah, judge, won't you please, between me and my vineyard.

What more could I do in <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads for}</sup> my vineyard, that I haven't already done? When I expected it to produce good <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack good}</sup> grapes, why did it yield <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read produce}</sup> wild ones? <sup>{I.e. grapes unsuitable for wine making}</sup>

"Now, let me tell you, won't you please, what I'm going to do to my vineyard. "I'm going to take away its protective hedge, and it will be devoured; <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT will be for devouring ; LXX will be for plundering}</sup> I'll break down its wall, and it will be trampled.
I'll make it a wasteland, and it won't be pruned or cultivated. Instead, briers and thorns will grow up. I'll also issue commands to the clouds, that they drop no rain upon it."

For the vineyard of the Lord of the Heavenly Armies is the house of Israel, and the men of Judah are the garden in which he delights. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT his delightful garden}</sup> He looked for justice, but saw only bloodshed; he searched <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack he searched}</sup> for righteousness, but heard only an outcry!

<sup>{Judgment on Land Barons}</sup> d "How terrible it will be for you who join house to house, who add field to field, until there is no more room, and you have settled yourselves alone <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads you are made to live alone}</sup> in the middle of the land!"
The Lord of the Heavenly Armies has declared this so I could hear it: "Surely many houses will become desolate, great and beautiful houses, without occupants.

For ten acres of vineyard will produce only six gallons, <sup>{Lit. only a bath}</sup> and ten bushels <sup>{Lit. and a homer}</sup> of seed <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack of seed}</sup> will produce only one bushel." <sup>{Lit. only an ephah}</sup>

<sup>{Judgment on Alcoholics}</sup> d "How terrible it will be for those who rise at dawn in order to grab <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX may run after}</sup> a stiff drink, for those who stay up late at night as wine inflames them!

They have the lyre and harp, the tambourine and flute, as well as wine at their festivals, but they don't respect what the Lord is doing, nor do they consider his actions. <sup>{Lit. consider the work of his hands}</sup>
Therefore my people go into exile because they lack understanding; my <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads their}</sup> honored men go hungry, and the crowd is parched with thirst.

Therefore Sheol's <sup>{I.e. the realm of the dead}</sup> appetite has grown; it has opened its mouth beyond limit. Jerusalem's nobility and her multitudes will go there, along with her brawlers and whoever is reveling within her.
Humanity is brought low, and each one is humbled, while the eyes of the self-exalting are brought low.
But the Lord of the Heavenly Armies is exalted in justice, and the Holy God proves himself to be righteously holy.
Then the lambs will graze in their pasture; fatlings and foreigners will eat among the waste places of the rich."
<sup>{Judgment on Mockers}</sup> d "How terrible it will be for those who parade iniquity with cords of falsehood, who draw sin along as <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack as}</sup> with a cart rope;

who say: «Let God <sup>{Lit. him}</sup> be quick, let him speed up <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads hurry}</sup> his work so we may see it! Let it happen! let the plan of the Holy One of Israel draw near, so we may recognize it!»"

<sup>{Judgment on Moral Relativists}</sup> d "How terrible it will be for those who call evil good and good evil, who substitute darkness for light and light for darkness, who substitute what is bitter for what is sweet and what is sweet for what is bitter!"
<sup>{Judgment on the Arrogant}</sup> d "How terrible it will be for those who are wise in their own opinion, and clever in their own reckoning!
"How terrible it will be for those who are heroes at drinking wine, and champions in mixing strong drink,
who acquit the guilty for a bribe, and deprive the innocent of justice!"

<sup>{The Effects of Divine Judgment}</sup> d Therefore, as flames of fire devour straw, as dry grass <sup>{So MT; 1QIsa a reads fire}</sup> collapses in flames, so their root will be rotten, and their blossom will blow away like dust, because they have rejected the instruction <sup>{Or law}</sup> of the Lord of the Heavenly Armies, and have despised the word of the Holy One of Israel.

Therefore <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; LXX reads And}</sup> the anger of the Lord <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; 4QIsa b LXX read Lord of the Heavenly Armies}</sup> burned against his people, so he stretched out his hands <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read hand}</sup> against them and afflicted them. The mountains quaked, and their corpses were like refuse in the middle of the streets. Throughout all of this, his anger has not turned away, and his hands are <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read his hand is}</sup> still stretched out to attack. <sup>{DSS MT lack to attack}</sup>

The Lord <sup>{Lit. He}</sup> will signal <sup>{Lit. will send up a signal}</sup> for nations far away, whistling for them to come <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack to come}</sup> from the ends of the earth. Look how quickly and how swiftly they come!

No one is weary, no one stumbles, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads stumbles among them}</sup> and no one slumbers or sleeps. No belt around their waists will come undone, nor will their sandal straps be broken.
Their arrows are sharp, all their bows ready for action. <sup>{Lit. bows bent}</sup> Their horses'hooves seem like flint, and their chariot wheels spin <sup>{DSS MT lack spin}</sup> like a whirlwind.

With a roar like a lion, they snarl, and like young lions, they growl; <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads Their roaring is like a lion; like young lions they roar. They growl}</sup> they seize their prey and then carry it off, with no one to rescue.

They will roar over it <sup>{I.e. over conquered Judah}</sup> at that time, <sup>{Lit. it on that day}</sup> like the sea waves roar. If one surveys the land, watch out! There's darkness and distress; even the daylight is darkened by its clouds.

<sup>{Holy is the Lord}</sup> d In the year that King Uzziah died, I saw the Lord sitting upon his <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read a}</sup> throne, high and exalted. The train of his robe filled the Temple.

The seraphim stood above him. Each had six wings: <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads six wings, six wings ; LXX reads six wings and six wings}</sup> with two he covered his face, and with two he covered his feet, and with two he was flying.

They kept on calling to each other: <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read calling and saying}</sup> "Holy, holy, holy <sup>{So MT LXX; 1QIsa a reads Holy, holy}</sup> is the Lord of the Heavenly Armies! The whole earth is full of his glory!"
The foundations of the thresholds quaked at the sound of those who kept calling out, <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads of him who called out}</sup> and the Temple was filled with smoke.

"How terrible it will be for me!" I cried, "because I am ruined! I'm a man with unclean lips, and I live among a people with unclean lips! And my eyes have seen the King, the Lord of the Heavenly Armies!"
<sup>{The Calling of Isaiah}</sup> d Then one of the seraphim flew to me, carrying a burning coal in his hand that he had taken from the altar with tongs.

He touched my mouth and said, "Look! Now that this has touched your <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT LXX; 4QIsa f reads the}</sup> lips, your guilt is taken away, and your sins <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads sin}</sup> atoned for."
Then I heard the voice of the Lord as he was asking, "Whom will I send? Who will go for us?" "Here I am!" I replied. "Send me."
"Go!" he responded. "Tell this people: «Keep on hearing, but do not understand; keep <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads and keep}</sup> on seeing, but do not perceive.'

Dull the mind <sup>{Lit. Fatten the heart}</sup> of this people, deafen their ears, and blind their eyes. By doing so, they won't see with their eyes, hear with their ears, understand with their minds, turn back, and be healed."

Then I asked, "For how long, Lord ?" <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads Lord}</sup> He replied: "Until cities lie waste, without inhabitants, and houses without people; and the land becomes utterly desolate.
Until <sup>{Lit. And}</sup> the Lord removes people far away, and there are many empty places in the middle of the land.

Even though a tenth of its people remain <sup>{Lit. tenth remains}</sup> in it, it will once again be burned, <sup>{Or devastated}</sup> like a terebinth <sup>{I.e. a sacred tree used for idolatry; cf. Hos 4:13}</sup> or an oak tree, <sup>{Or Asherah pole ; i.e. felled oaks used for making idols; cf. Hos 4:13, Isa 44:14}</sup> the stump of which, though the tree has been <sup>{1QIsa a lacks though the tree has been ; MT LXX read which, when}</sup> felled, still contains holy seed." <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; LXX lacks this line}</sup>

<sup>{The Message to Ahaz}</sup> d During the reign of Jotham's son Ahaz, Uzziah's grandson, king of Judah, King Rezin of Aram and Remaliah's son Pekah, king of Israel, approached Jerusalem and waged war against it, but they <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads he}</sup> could not mount an attack against it.

When it was reported to the house of David, "Aram has joined forces with Ephraim!" the <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read his}</sup> heart of the people of Ahaz <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read his heart and the heart of his people}</sup> trembled like forest <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks forest}</sup> trees in a windstorm.
So the Lord told Isaiah, "Go out to meet Ahaz, you and your son Shear-jashub, at the end of the aqueduct of the Upper Pool that proceeds along the highway to Launderer's Field.

Tell him, «Be careful, be calm, don't be afraid, and don't lose heart because of these two smoldering stumps of torches, that is, because of <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks because of}</sup> the fierce anger of Rezin, from Aram, and Remaliah's son.
Aram, Ephraim, and Remaliah's son have plotted this evil against you:
"Let's go attack Judah, let's terrorize it, and let's conquer it for ourselves. Then we'll install Tabeel's son as king!"'
«But this is what the Lord God has to say: «It won't take place. It won't ever happen.
Because Aram's head is Damascus, and Rezin is its king, <sup>{Lit. is head of Damascus}</sup> within sixty-five years Ephraim will be shattered as a people.

Furthermore, Ephraim's head is Samaria, and Remaliah's son is its king. <sup>{Lit. is head of Samaria}</sup> If all of you don't keep on believing, you'll never remain loyal.»" <sup>{Or never keep on enduring}</sup>
<sup>{God with Us}</sup> d Later on, the Lord spoke to Ahaz again:
"Ask a sign from the Lord your God. Make it as deep as Sheol <sup>{I.e. the realm of the dead}</sup> or as high as heaven above."
But Ahaz replied, "I won't ask! I won't put the Lord to the test."

In reply, the Lord <sup>{Lit. reply, he}</sup> announced, "Please listen, you household of David. Is it such a minor thing for you to try the patience of <sup>{Lit. to wear out}</sup> men? Must you also try the patience of <sup>{Lit. also wear out}</sup> my God?

"Therefore the Lord <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads Lord}</sup> himself will give you a sign. Watch! The virgin <sup>{So LXX; 1QIsa a MT read The young woman}</sup> is conceiving a child, and will give birth to a son, and his name will be called <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read she will name him ; MT alt. reading and you will name him}</sup> Immanuel. <sup>{The Heb. name Immanuel means God with us}</sup>
He'll eat cheese <sup>{Or curds}</sup> and honey, when he knows enough to reject what's wrong and choose what's right.
However, before the youth knows enough to reject what's wrong and choose what's right, the land whose two kings you dread will be devastated."

<sup>{Conquest by Assyria}</sup> d "The Lord will bring to you, to your people, and to your ancestor's house such a time <sup>{Lit. such days}</sup> as has never been since Ephraim broke away from Judah the king of Assyria will come. <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack will come}</sup>

"At that time, <sup>{Lit. On that day}</sup> the Lord will call <sup>{Lit. whistle}</sup> for flies that will come from far away from the headwaters of Egypt's rivers and for bees that are in the land of Assyria.
They will all come and settle in the steep ravines, in the rocky crevices, in all the thorn bushes, and in all the pastures. <sup>{Or the watering places}</sup>

At that time, <sup>{Lit. On that day}</sup> the Lord will hire a barber <sup>{Lit. razor}</sup> to come <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack to come}</sup> from beyond the Euphrates <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack Euphrates}</sup> River that is, the king of Assyria and he will shave your heads, your leg <sup>{Or feet}</sup> hair, and your beards, too.
"At that time, <sup>{Lit. On that day}</sup> a man will keep alive a heifer and two sheep,
and because of the abundance of milk that they give, he will have cheese <sup>{Or curds}</sup> to eat, since whoever remains in the land will be eating cheese <sup>{Or curds}</sup> and honey.
"At that time, <sup>{Lit. On that day}</sup> every place where once there were a thousand vines worth a thousand shekels of silver, only briars and thorns will grow.
"People will come there armed with bows <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read bow}</sup> and arrows, because the entire land will be nothing but briers and thorns.

As for all the hills that used to be cultivated with a hoe, you won't go there, because you'll fear iron <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack iron}</sup> briars and thorns. Nevertheless, those hills <sup>{Lit. but they}</sup> will be reserved as a pasture where cattle will feed and where sheep will graze." <sup>{Lit. tread}</sup>

<sup>{Isaiah's Son is Born}</sup> d The Lord also told me, "Take a large tablet and write on it with a stylus <sup>{Or with an ordinary}</sup> pen, «For Maher-shalal-hash-baz'. <sup>{The Heb. name Maher-shalal-hash-baz means Hurry to the plunder, quick to the loot}</sup>
Then I will call <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads call in}</sup> Uriah the priest and Jeberechiah's son Zechariah as reliable witnesses to testify on my behalf."

After this, I was intimate with the prophetess and she conceived. Later, she bore a son, and then the Lord told me, <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT LXX; 4QIsa e lacks me}</sup> "Call him <sup>{Lit. Call his name}</sup> «Maher-shalal-hash-baz,'

for before the young lad knows how to call out to his father or mother, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads out «My father!'or «My mother!'; LXX reads «Father!'or «Mother!'}</sup> the wealth of Damascus and the plunder of Samaria will be carried off by the king of Assyria."
<sup>{Invasion by Assyria}</sup> d The Lord spoke to me again:
"Because this people have rejected the gently-flowing waters of Shiloah, and because <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack because}</sup> they keep rejoicing in Rezin and Remaliah's son,

watch out! The Lord God <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads the Lord}</sup> is about to bring the flood waters of the Euphrates <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack Euphrates}</sup> River against them, mighty and strong. <sup>{So 4QIsa f MT LXX; 1QIsa a lacks mighty and strong}</sup> "It's the king of Assyria and all of his arrogance! He will rise over all of the river's channels and run over all of its banks.

He will sweep on into Judah, overflowing as he passes through, like flood waters <sup>{DSS MT lack like flood waters}</sup> reaching up to a person's neck. His outstretched wings will flow as wide as your land, O Immanuel!"

"Band together, <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; 4QIsa e 4QIsa f LXX read Learn this ; or Know this}</sup> you peoples, but be shattered! Listen, all you distant countries! Strap on your armor, but be shattered. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT adds a second strap on your armor but be shattered ; cf. LXX}</sup>

Take counsel together, but it will all be for nothing; go ahead and talk, but <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; 4QIsa e LXX lack but}</sup> it will all be for nothing, <sup>{Lit. it won't stand}</sup> for God is with us." <sup>{I.e. a word play on the name Immanuel ; cf. 7:14, 8:8}</sup>

<sup>{Waiting on God}</sup> d For <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa e MT; 4QIsa f LXX Syr lack For}</sup> this is what the Lord spoke to me, as his forceful hand was resting on me, and as he was warning me not to live the way this people were living: <sup>{Lit. not to walk in the way of this people}</sup>
"Don't call conspiracy everything that this people calls conspiracy, and don't fear what they fear, or live in terror.
The Lord of the Heavenly Armies he's the one you are to regard as holy. Let him be the one whom you fear, and let him be the one before whom you stand in terror!
Then he will be a sanctuary, but for both houses of Israel he'll also be a stone with which someone strikes himself, a rock one stumbles over, a trap and a snare to those who live in Jerusalem.
Many will stumble on them; They'll fall and be broken; They'll be snared and captured.
"Bind up the testimony, and seal up the teaching among my disciples.
I'll wait for the Lord , who is hiding his face from the house of Jacob, and I'll put my trust in him.

Watch out! I and the children whom the Lord has given me are a sign and a wonder <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read are signs and wonders}</sup> in Israel from the Lord of the Heavenly Armies, who resides on Mount Zion."

<sup>{Rejecting Occultic Wisdom}</sup> d "So when they advise you, «Ask the mediums your questions, and quiz the spiritists who chirp and mutter,'shouldn't a people instead be consulting their God <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads gods}</sup> and not the dead on behalf of those who are living
for instruction and for testimony? Surely they are speaking like this because the truth <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack the truth}</sup> hasn't dawned on them.

"They'll pass through the land, <sup>{Lit. through it}</sup> while <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks while}</sup> greatly distressed and hungry. When they are hungry, they'll become enraged, and they'll curse their king and their god. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads gods ; LXX reads idols}</sup> They'll turn their faces upwards,

or they'll look toward the <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; the Heb. lacks the}</sup> earth, but they'll see only distress and darkness, the gloom that comes from anguish, and then they'll be thrown into total darkness."

<sup>{The Prince of Peace}</sup> d <sup>{This v. is 8:23 in the MT}</sup> But there will be no gloom for her who was <sup>{So DSS; MT reads those who were}</sup> in distress. Formerly, he brought contempt to the region of Zebulun and the region of Naphtali, but in the future <sup>{So DSS; MT reads the latter time}</sup> he will have made glorious the way of the sea, the territory beyond the Jordan Galilee of the nations. <sup>{Or gentiles}</sup>
<sup>{This v. is 9:1 in the MT}</sup> The people who walked in darkness have seen a great light; for those living in a land of deep darkness, a light has shined upon them.

You have increased the nation; you have increased its joy; they rejoice in your presence as they rejoice at the harvest, as they are glad when they're dividing the spoils of war. <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack of war}</sup>

Now as to the yoke that has been <sup>{Lit. yoke of}</sup> his burden, and the bar laid <sup>{DSS The Heb. lacks laid}</sup> on his shoulder the rod of his oppressor you have broken it <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack it}</sup> as on the day of Midiam. <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads Midian ; cf. Judg 7:8-25 2King 15:19; 16:8}</sup>
For every boot of the tramping soldier in battle tumult and every garment rolled in blood will be used for burning as fuel for a fire.

For to us a child is born, to us a son is given; and the government will be upon his shoulder, and his name is <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT 4QIsa c read name will be}</sup> called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.

Of the growth of his government and peace there will be no end. He will rule <sup>{DSS MT lack He will rule}</sup> over his kingdom, sitting on the throne of David, to establish it and to uphold it <sup>{So 1QIsa a , referring to the throne; MT reads it , referring to the kingdom}</sup> with justice and righteousness from this time onward and forevermore. The zeal of the Lord of the Heavenly Armies will accomplish this.

<sup>{A Rebuke to Jacob and Israel}</sup> d "The Lord <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads Lord}</sup> has sent a plague <sup>{So LXX; MT reads word ; 1QIsa a can mean plague or word .}</sup> against Jacob, and it will fall on Israel;
and all of the people were evil <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read knew}</sup> Ephraim and the inhabitants of Samaria saying proudly with arrogant hearts:
«The bricks have fallen, but we will build with dressed stones; the sycamore trees have been cut down, but we will replace them with cedars.'
But the Lord has raised adversaries <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT LXX; other MT mss. read princes}</sup> from Rezin <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; LXX lacks from Rezin}</sup> against him, and he stirs up his enemies

Arameans from the east and Philistines from the west and they devour Israel with open mouths! "Yet <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa c ; the Heb. lacks Yet}</sup> for all this, his anger has not turned away, and his hand is still stretched out, ready to strike." <sup>{DSS MT lack ready to strike}</sup>

<sup>{Judgment for Not Repenting}</sup> d "But the people have not returned to rely <sup>{1QIsa a MT LXX lack to rely}</sup> on <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads toward ; LXX reads until}</sup> him who struck them, nor have they sought the Lord of the Heavenly Armies.
So the Lord has cut off from Israel head and tail, palm branch and reed in <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; the The Heb. lacks in}</sup> a single day
the elder and the dignitary is the head, and the prophet who teaches lies is the tail.
For those who guide this people have been leading them astray, and those who are guided by them are swallowed up.

Therefore the Lord does not have pity on <sup>{So 1QIsa a ;MT LXX read rejoice over}</sup> their young men, and has no compassion on their orphans <sup>{Or fatherless}</sup> and widows, because each of them was godless and an evildoer, and every mouth spoke folly. "Yet <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa c ; the Heb. lacks Yet}</sup> for all this, his anger has not turned away, and his hand is still stretched out, ready to strike. <sup>{DSS MT lack ready to strike}</sup>
"For wickedness has burned like a blaze that consumes briers and thorns; it sets thickets of the forest on fire, and skyward <sup>{Lit. upward}</sup> they swirl in a column of smoke.
From <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads By}</sup> the wrath of the Lord of the Heavenly Armies the land has been scorched, and the people have become like fuel for the fire; no one will spare his neighbor.

They cut meat on the right, but they're still hungry, and they devour also <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lacks also}</sup> on the left, but they're not satisfied; each devours the flesh of his own children. <sup>{So 4QIsa e ; or arms ; 1QIsa a MT read offsprin g or arm ; LXX reads arm}</sup>

Manasseh devours Ephraim, and Ephraim devours Manasseh; together they are against Judah. "Yet <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack Yet}</sup> for all this, his anger has not turned away, and his hand is still stretched out, ready to strike." <sup>{DSS MT lack ready to strike}</sup>

<sup>{Judgment on Unjust Lawmakers}</sup> d "How terrible it will be for the one <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads the ones}</sup> who enacts unjust decrees, for those who write oppressive laws that they have prescribed

to deprive the needy of justice and to rob the poor of my people of their rights, <sup>{Lit. right}</sup> so that widows may become their spoil and so that they may plunder orphans! <sup>{Or plunder the fatherless}</sup>
What will you do on the day of Judgment, <sup>{Lit. reckoning}</sup> in the calamity that will come from far away? To whom will you run for help, and where will you leave your wealth,

so you won't have to crouch among those in chains <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read beneath prisoners}</sup> or fall among the slain? "Yet <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack Yet}</sup> for all this, his anger has not turned away, and his hand is still stretched out, ready to strike." <sup>{DSS MT lack ready to strike}</sup>
<sup>{Assyria is an Instrument of Judgment}</sup> d "How terrible it will be for Assyria, the rod of my anger! The club is in their hands! <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads is their fury!}</sup>

I'm sending my fury <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads sending him}</sup> against a godless nation, and I'll command him against the people with whom I'm angry to seize loot and snatch plunder, and to trample them down like mud in the streets.
But this is not what he intends, and this is not what he thinks in his mind; but it is in his mind to destroy, and to cut down <sup>{Lit. off}</sup> many nations.
"Because this is what he is saying: «My commanders are all kings, are they not?
Isn't Calno like Carchemish? Isn't Hamath like Arpad? Isn't Samaria like Damascus?
As my hand has reached to the idolatrous kingdoms <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads the idol}</sup> whose carved images were greater than those of Jerusalem and Samaria,
will I not deal with Jerusalem and her idols as I have dealt with Samaria and her images?»"

<sup>{Assyria will be Judged}</sup> d "For <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read And when}</sup> the Lord has finished all his work against Mount Zion and against Jerusalem; he will punish the speech that comes from that willful <sup>{Lit. the fruit of the arrogant}</sup> heart of Assyria's king and the haughty look in his eyes.

He keeps bragging: <sup>{Lit. saying ; so 1QIsa a ; MT reads He said}</sup> «I've done it by the strength of my hand, and by my wisdom, because I'm so clever. <sup>{Lit. I have understanding}</sup> I removed the boundaries of peoples, and plundered their treasures; like a bull I brought down those who sat on thrones.

My hand has found, as if in a nest, the wealth of the people; and as one gathers eggs that have been abandoned, so I have gathered all the inhabitants of the <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack inhabitants of the}</sup> earth. Nothing moved a wing, opened its mouth, or chirped.'

"Does the ax exalt itself over the one who swings it? Or does the saw magnify itself in opposition to the one who wields it? As if a rod were to wield those who lift <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; LXX reads the one who lifts}</sup> it, or as if a club were to brandish the one who is not wood!

Therefore, the Lord God <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; other LXX MT mss read Lord}</sup> of the Heavenly Armies will send a wasting disease among Assyria's <sup>{Lit. his}</sup> sturdy warriors, and under its glory a conflagration will be kindled, like a blazing bonfire.
"The light of Israel will become a fire, and its Holy One a flame, and it will burn and consume Assyria's <sup>{Lit. its}</sup> thorns and briers in a single day.
The splendor of its forest and its fruitful land the Lord will destroy both soul and body and Assyria <sup>{Lit. it}</sup> will be as when a dying man wastes away.
What survives of the trees in his forest will be so few that a child can count them." <sup>{Lit. can write them down}</sup>

<sup>{The Remnant Returns}</sup> d At that time, the remnant of Israel and the survivors of the house of Jacob will no longer rely on the one who struck them down, but will truly rely on the Lord , the Holy One of Israel.
A remnant will return a remnant of Jacob to the Mighty God.
For even if your people of Israel number as many as the sand of the sea, only a remnant of them will return. Overwhelming, righteous destruction is decreed,

because the Lord God of the Heavenly Armies <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; LXX, MT mss lack Lord of the Heavenly Armies}</sup> will bring about destruction, as has been decreed, throughout <sup>{Lit. in the midst of}</sup> the entire region. <sup>{Lit. land}</sup>

Therefore this is what the Lord God of the Heavenly Armies says: "My people, you who live in Zion, don't be afraid of the Assyrians, of the rod that beats you, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads Assyrians, when they strike you with a rod}</sup> who lift up their club against you as the Egyptians did.
In just a little while, my fury will come to an end, and my anger then will be directed to their destruction. <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; MT mss. read end ; LXX reads counsel}</sup>

The Lord of the Heavenly Armies will brandish a whip against them, as when he struck Midian at the rock of Oreb; <sup>{Cf. Judg 7:25}</sup> and as his staff was stretched out <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack stretched out}</sup> over the sea, <sup>{Cf. Exod 14:16,26}</sup> so he will lift it up as he did in Egypt.

At that time, <sup>{Lit. On that day ; so 1QIsa a MT LXX; 4QIsa c reads On a day}</sup> his burden will depart from your shoulder and his yoke from your neck. Indeed, the yoke will be broken, because you've become obese." <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; LXX lacks because you've become obese .}</sup>

<sup>{The Coming Judgment of God}</sup> d "The Assyrian commander <sup>{Lit. He}</sup> has come upon <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; 4QIsa c LXX read to}</sup> Aiath and has passed through Migron; he stores his supplies at Michmash.
He has <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads They have}</sup> crossed over by <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks by}</sup> the pass; his overnight lodging is at Geba. Ramah trembles; Gibeah of Saul has fled.
Cry aloud, you daughter of Gallim! Pay attention, Laish! <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read Laishah}</sup> Poor Anathoth!
Marmenah <sup>{So 1QIsa a Syr.; MT LXX read Madmenah}</sup> is in flight; the inhabitants of Gebim take cover.

This very day he will halt at Nob; <sup>{I.e. city where the ephod was stored during the reign of Saul; cf. 1Sam 22:13-20}</sup> he will shake <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads brandish}</sup> his fists <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads fist}</sup> at the mountain that is the Daughter of Zion, at Jerusalem's hill.
Behold, the Lord God of the Heavenly Armies will lop off its <sup>{Lit. the}</sup> boughs with terrifying power; the tallest in height will be cut down, and the lofty will be brought low.
He will cut down the thickets of the forest with an ax, and Lebanon will fall by the Majestic One." <sup>{Or fall, along with its majestic trees}</sup>
<sup>{The Reign of the Davidic King}</sup> d "A shoot will come out from the stump of Jesse, and a branch will bear fruit from his roots.
The Spirit of the Lord will rest upon him, the Spirit of wisdom and understanding, the Spirit of counsel and power, the Spirit of knowledge and fear of the Lord .
His delight will be in the fear of the Lord . He won't judge by what his eyes see, nor decide disputes by what his ears hear,

but with righteousness he will judge the needy, and decide with equity for <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read for the}</sup> earth's poor. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read humble}</sup> He will strike the earth with the rod of his mouth, <sup>{I.e. by pronouncing judgment}</sup> and the wicked will be killed <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa a corrector MT LXX read he will kill the wicked}</sup> with the breath of his lips.
Righteousness will be the sash around his loins, and faithfulness the belt around his waist."

<sup>{A Transformed Ecology}</sup> d "The wolf will live with the lamb; the leopard will lie down with the young goat. The calf and the lion will graze <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads lion and the fattened calf}</sup> together, and a little child will lead them.
The cow and the bear will graze, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> their young will lie down together, and the lion will eat straw like the ox.
The nursing child will play over the hole of the cobra, and the weaned child will put his hand on vipers'dens. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads a viper's den ; LXX reads a den of vipers}</sup>

They will neither harm nor destroy on <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; 4QIsa c MT read on all}</sup> my holy mountain; for the earth will be full of the knowledge <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; 4QIsa c LXX read to know}</sup> of the Lord, <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT LXX; 4QIsa c reads of glory ; cf. Hab 2:14}</sup> as the waters cover the sea."

<sup>{Israel Regathered}</sup> d At that time, <sup>{Lit. day}</sup> as to <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack as to}</sup> the root of Jesse, who will be standing as a banner for the peoples, the nations will rally to him, and his resting place is <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa c MT LXX read place will be}</sup> glorious.

At that time, <sup>{Lit. day}</sup> the Lord will reach out his hand yet a second time to recover the remnant that is left of his people, from Assyria, from Lower Egypt, from Upper Egypt, <sup>{Lit. from Egypt, from Pathros}</sup> from Cush, from Elam, from Shinar, from Hamath, and from the islands <sup>{Or coastlands}</sup> of the sea.

He will raise a banner for the nations and will assemble the dispersed of Israel; he will gather the scattered people of Judah from the corners <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa a MT LXX read four corners}</sup> of the earth.

<sup>{Israel's Victory over Its Enemies}</sup> d Ephraim's jealousy will vanish, <sup>{Lit. depart}</sup> and those who are hostile to Judah will be eliminated; <sup>{Lit. be cut off}</sup> Ephraim will no longer be jealous of Judah, and Judah will not be hostile to Ephraim.

But they <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT LXX; 4QIsa a Targ read he}</sup> will swoop down on the slopes <sup>{Lit. backs}</sup> of the Philistines to the west, and they will plunder <sup>{So pap4QIsa e 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read the west; together they will plunder}</sup> the people to <sup>{Lit. of}</sup> the east. They'll lay their hands on Edom and Moab, and the Ammonites will be subject to them.

The Lord will totally destroy the gulf <sup>{Lit. tongue}</sup> of the Sea of Egypt. He will sweep his hand over the Euphrates River with a violent wind, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read his violent wind}</sup> breaking it up into seven streams, and making a way for people to cross on foot.
And there will be a highway for the remnant that is left of his people out of Assyria, as there was for Israel when they came up from the land of Egypt.

<sup>{Israel's Praise to the Lord}</sup> d At that time, <sup>{Lit. day}</sup> you will say: "I will praise you, Lord , for although you were angry with me, your anger has turned away, and you have comforted me.

"Look! God yes God is <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads Look! God is ; LXX reads Look! The Lord is the God of}</sup> my salvation; I will trust, and not be afraid. For the Lord <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT mss LXX; MT reads Lord God}</sup> is my strength and my song, <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT mss LXX; MT reads a song}</sup> and he has become my salvation."
You will draw water joyfully from the wells of salvation. And you will say at that time: <sup>{Lit. say in that day}</sup>
"Give thanks to the Lord; call on his name. Make known his actions among the nations. Proclaim that his name is exalted.

"Sing praises to the Lord , <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads to the Lord ; LXX reads to the name of the Lord}</sup> because he has acted gloriously, being made <sup>{So Isa a MT qere Syr Targ; the Heb. lacks made}</sup> known in all the world.
Shout aloud, and sing for joy, you who live in Zion, because great in your midst is the Holy One of Israel."
<sup>{The Destruction of Babylon}</sup> d A message <sup>{Lit. An oracle}</sup> that Amoz's son Isaiah received <sup>{Lit. saw}</sup> about Babylon:

"Raise a banner on a bare hilltop! Cry out loud to them! Give a wave of the hand, signaling <sup>{The Heb. lacks signaling}</sup> for them to enter <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads for them to enter ; LXX lacks to enter}</sup> the gates of the nobles.
I myself have commanded my consecrated ones; I have also summoned my warriors, those who rejoice in my triumph, to carry out my angry judgments. <sup>{Lit. my anger}</sup>

"Listen! There's a noise on the mountains like that of a great multitude! Listen! There's an uproar among the kingdoms, like that of nations massing together! The Lord of the Heavenly Armies is mustering an army for battle.
They're coming from a faraway land, from the distant horizon <sup>{Lit. end of the heavens}</sup> the Lord and the weapons of his anger to destroy the entire land." <sup>{Or earth}</sup>
<sup>{The Day of the Lord}</sup> d Wail out loud, because the Day of the Lord is near. It will come like destruction from the Almighty!
Because of this, every hand <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads all hands}</sup> will go limp, and every man's courage <sup>{Lit. heart}</sup> will melt.

They will be terrified; pain and anguish will seize them; they'll writhe like a woman in labor. They'll look aghast at one another; and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; cf. LXX; 4QIsa a 4QIsa b MT lack and}</sup> their faces will be ablaze with fear. <sup>{DSS MT lack with fear}</sup>

Watch out! The Day of the Lord is coming cruel, with wrath and fierce anger to turn the entire inhabited <sup>{So LXX; 1QIsa a lacks the entire inhabited ; the Heb. lacks entire inhabited}</sup> earth <sup>{LXX lacks earth}</sup> into a desolation and to annihilate sinners <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; 4QIsa a 4QIsa b MT read its sinners}</sup> from it.
For the stars of the heavens and their constellations won't shine <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads beam}</sup> their light; the sun will be dark when it rises, and the moon won't shine its light.
I'll punish the world for its evil, and the wicked for their iniquity; I'll put an end to the pomposity of the arrogant, and overthrow the insolence of tyrants.
I'll make people scarcer <sup>{Lit. people more precious}</sup> than pure gold, and mankind rarer <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack rarer}</sup> than gold from Ophir.

Therefore I'll make the heavens tremble. The earth will shake from its place at the wrath of the Lord of the Heavenly Armies, at the time <sup>{Lit. in the day}</sup> of his burning anger. <sup>{Lit. nostrils}</sup>

They <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads it}</sup> will be like a hunted gazelle, or like sheep with no one to gather them, <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT LXX; 4QIsa a reads banished}</sup> each will turn to his own people, and each will flee to his own land.
Whoever is captured will be thrust through, and whoever is caught will fall dead, killed <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT lacks dead, killed}</sup> by the sword.

Their infants will be dashed to pieces before their eyes, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a Syr; cf. LXX; 4QIsa a MT lack and}</sup> their houses will be looted, and their wives slept with. <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT qere ; 4QIsa a MT read raped ; LXX reads they will take ; cf. Deut 28:30}</sup>
<sup>{Babylon Falls}</sup> d Watch out! I'm stirring up the Medes against them, who care nothing for silver and take no delight in gold.

Their bows will dash the young men to pieces; they'll show no pity on those not yet born, <sup>{Lit. on the fruit of the womb}</sup> and <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT mss LXX; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> their eyes will not spare children.
Babylon, that jewel of kingdoms, the splendor and pride of the Chaldeans, will be like Sodom and Gomorrah, when God overthrew them

It will never be inhabited or lived in through all generations; no Bedouin <sup>{I.e. Middle Eastern nomadic herders; or Arab}</sup> will pitch his tent there; no shepherds will make their flocks lie down there.

But desert beasts will lie down there, and their houses will be full of howling creatures; there owls <sup>{Or ostriches}</sup> will dwell, and goat-demons <sup>{Or satyrs ; or wild goats}</sup> will dance there.

Hyenas will howl in its strongholds, <sup>{Lit. desolate places}</sup> and jackals will make their dens in its citadels. <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads in the citadels of luxury}</sup> Its <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads And its}</sup> time is close at hand, and its days will not be extended any further. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack any further}</sup>

<sup>{Israel Mocks Babylon's King}</sup> d However, the Lord will have compassion on Jacob and will once again choose Israel. He will settle them in their own land, and foreigners will join them, affiliating themselves with the house of Jacob.

Many <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack Many}</sup> nations will take them and bring them to their land and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read and to}</sup> their own place. The house of Israel will put those nations <sup>{Lit. put them}</sup> to conscripted labor <sup>{Lit. to male and female slavery}</sup> in the Lord's land. They will take captive those who were their captors, and will rule continually <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa c LXX MT read rule}</sup> over those who oppressed them.

At the time, <sup>{Lit. day}</sup> when the Lord gives you rest from your suffering, turmoil, and the cruel bondage which they forced you to serve, <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa e ; MT LXX read which you were forced to serve}</sup>

you will lift up this song of mockery against <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT LXX; 4QIsa e reads to}</sup> the king of Babylon: "How the oppressor has come to an end! How the attacker <sup>{1QIsa a LXX; MT reads. the golden city}</sup> has ceased!
The Lord has broken the staff of the wicked, the scepter of rulers,
that struck down peoples in anger with unceasing blows, that oppressed nations in fury with relentless persecution.
The entire earth is at rest and peace; its inhabitants <sup>{Lit. they}</sup> break into song.

Even the cypresses rejoice over you, as do <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack as do}</sup> the cedars of Lebanon, saying, «Now that you've been laid low, no woodcutter comes up against us.'<sup>{So 1QIsa a MT LXX; 4QIsa e reads against them}</sup>

"The afterlife <sup>{Lit. Sheol , i.e. the realm of the dead}</sup> below is all astir to meet you when you arrive; <sup>{Lit. at your coming}</sup> it rouses up the spirits of the dead to greet you everyone who used to be world leaders. It has raised up from their thrones all who used to be kings of the nations.
In answer, all of them <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT LXX; 4QIsa e lacks of them}</sup> will tell you, «You've also become as weak as we are! You have become just like us!'

Your <sup>{1QIsa a reads The; MT LXX lack Your}</sup> pomp has been brought down to Sheol, <sup>{I.e. the realm of the dead}</sup> along with the noise of your harps. Maggots are spread out beneath you, and worms are your covering." <sup>{So LXX; MT reads and a worm covers you}</sup>

<sup>{The Fall of the Day Star}</sup> d "How you have fallen from heaven, Day Star, son of the Dawn! <sup>{I.e. Lucifer}</sup> How you have been thrown down to earth, you who laid low the nation! <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa e MT read the nations ; LXX reads all the nations}</sup>

You said in your heart, «I'll ascend to heaven, above the stars of God. I'll erect my throne; I'll sit <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads and I will sit}</sup> on the Mount of Assembly in the far reaches of the north; <sup>{Lit. Zaphon ; or the Sacred Mountain}</sup>
I'll ascend above the tops of the clouds; I'll make myself like the Most High.'
But you are brought down to join the dead, <sup>{Lit. to Sheol , i.e. the realm of the dead}</sup> to the far reaches of the Pit. <sup>{I.e. the realm of punishment in the afterlife}</sup>
"Those who see you will stare at you. They will wonder about you: «Is this the man who <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; the Heb. lacks who}</sup> made the earth tremble, who made kingdoms quake,
who made the world like a desert, who <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read and who}</sup> destroyed its cities, who would not open the jails for his prisoners? '
All the kings of the nations lie <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads nations, every one of them lies}</sup> in state, each in his own tomb.

But you are cast away from your grave, like a repulsive branch, your clothing is the slain, those pierced by the sword; those who go down to the Pit. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; i.e. to the realm of punishment in the afterlife; LXX reads to Hades ; 1QIsa a corrector MT read to the stones of the Pit}</sup> Like a dead body trampled underfoot,

you will not be united <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; Lit. under ; MT reads joined}</sup> with them <sup>{I.e. with the dead}</sup> in burial, for you have destroyed your land, you have slain your people. People will never mention the descendants of those who practice evil again! <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads May the descendants of those who practice of evil never be mentioned again! ; LXX reads May you not remain forever, you evil seed!}</sup>
Prepare a massacre for his sons because of the guilt of their forefathers! They are not to rise and inherit the earth, and cover <sup>{Lit. fill}</sup> the surface of the world with cities."

<sup>{Babylon's Desolation}</sup> d "I will rise up against them,"declares the Lord of the Heavenly Armies, "and I will eliminate from Babylon her name and survivors, her offspring and descendants,"<sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads and her offspring and descendants ; LXX lacks and descendants}</sup> declares the Lord .

"And <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack And}</sup> I'm going to make it a possession of the hedgehog pools <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads and pools}</sup> of water and I'll sweep <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads sweep it}</sup> with the broom of destruction,"declares the Lord of the Heavenly Armies.

The Lord of the Heavenly Armies has sworn: "Surely as I have planned, that's what she <sup>{I.e. Babylon}</sup> will become; <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads so has she been}</sup> and just as I have determined, so will it remain

to crush the Assyrian in my land, and on my mountains I will trample him down. His yoke <sup>{I.e. Assyria's oppressive domination}</sup> will turn away from you, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read from them}</sup> and his burden from your <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads their}</sup> shoulders."
"This is what I've planned for the whole earth, and this is the hand that is stretched out over all the nations.
For the Lord of the Heavenly Armies has planned, and who can thwart him? His hand is stretched out, and who can turn it back?"
<sup>{A Pronouncement against Philistia}</sup> d In the year that King Ahaz died this message <sup>{Lit. oracle}</sup> came:

"Don't rejoice, all of you Philistines, that the rod that struck you is broken, because from the snake's root a viper will spring up, and its offspring <sup>{Lit. fruit}</sup> will be a darting, poisonous serpent.

The firstborn of the poor will find pasture, and the needy will lie down in safety; but I'll kill your root <sup>{I.e. the source of their strengths}</sup> by famine, and I'll <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read he}</sup> execute your survivors.

Wail, you gate! Cry out, you city! Melt away, <sup>{Or melt in fear}</sup> all you Philistines! For smoke comes from the north, and there's no one to take measure <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa o MT read no straggler}</sup> in its festivals. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa o reads ranks}</sup>

How will they <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads one}</sup> answer the messengers of the nation? "The Lord has founded Zion, and in it <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads her}</sup> the afflicted among his people will find refuge."

<sup>{Moab's Pending Judgment}</sup> d A message <sup>{Lit. An oracle}</sup> concerning Moab: "For Ir <sup>{Or For the city ; so 1QIsa a ; MT reads Ar ; LXX lacks For Ir}</sup> in Moab is destroyed in a night, and Moab <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads it; LXX lacks it}</sup> is ruined! Because Ir <sup>{Or Because the city ; so 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa o MT LXX read Because the wall or Because Kir}</sup> in Moab is destroyed in a single night, Moab is ruined!

He has gone up to the temple, and to Dibon, to the high places to weep; over Nebo and over Medeba Moab wails. His head is completely <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads all its heads are ; MT mss LXX read Over every head ; cf. Jer 48:37}</sup> bald, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a Mt mss LXX; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> every beard is shaved off.
In its streets they wear sackcloth; on its rooftops and in its squares everyone wails and falls down <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads falling down ; LXX lacks and falls down}</sup> weeping.
Heshbon and Elealeh cry out, their voices are heard as far as Jahaz; therefore the loins <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; cf. LXX; MT reads armed men}</sup> of Moab cry aloud; its heart quakes for itself.
My heart cries out over Moab; her fugitives flee as far as Zoar, as far as Eglath-shelishiyah. For at the ascent to Luhith they go up weeping; on the road to Horonaim they raise a cry of destruction.
The Nimrim waters are desolate; the grass is withered, its vegetation gone; there is <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads was or there has been ; LXX reads there will be}</sup> no foliage left.

Therefore the wealth they have acquired and what they have stored up they carry them away over the Arab <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; cf. LXX; MT reads Willow}</sup> Wadi. <sup>{I.e. a seasonal stream or river that channels water during rain seasons but is dry at other times}</sup>
For the cry has gone out along the border of Moab; her wailing reaches as far as Eglaim, her wailing reaches as far as Beer-elim.

The Dibon <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads Dimon ; LXX reads Remmon}</sup> streams are full of blood; but I will bring upon Dibon <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads Dimon ; LXX reads Remmon}</sup> even more a lion will pounce <sup>{1QIsa a LXX MT lack will pounce}</sup> upon those of Moab who escape, upon <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; the Heb. lacks upon}</sup> those who remain in the land."

<sup>{Moab's Destruction}</sup> d "Send a lamb to the ruler of the land, from Selah, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads Sela ; LXX reads not the rock}</sup> by way of the desert, to the mountain of the Daughter of Zion. <sup>{I.e. Mt. Zion}</sup>
Like fluttering birds, like an abandoned nest, so are the daughters of Moab at the fords of the Arnon River. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks River}</sup>
"Give us advice; reach a decision! Cast your shadow as if night had come at high noon. Shelter the fugitives, And don't betray a single refugee.

Let the fugitives <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads my fugitives}</sup> from Moab settle among you; be a shelter to them from the destroyer. When the oppressor comes to an end, and destruction has <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads have}</sup> ceased, and the marauder <sup>{Lit. the one who tramples underfoot}</sup> has <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads have}</sup> vanished from the land,
then a throne will be established in gracious love, and there will sit in faithfulness in the Tent of David one who judges, seeks justice, and is swift to do what is right."

"We've heard about Moab's pride so very proud he became! <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT mss ; cf. Jer 48:29; MT reads how very proud he was}</sup> his arrogance, his pride, and his insolence; therefore he is alone. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read but his boasts mean nothing}</sup>

Therefore, let Moab not <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks not}</sup> wail, let everyone wail for Moab. Lament and grieve deeply for the ruined remains <sup>{Or for the raisin cakes}</sup> of Kir-hareseth.

For the fields of Heshbon wither, as well as the vines of Sibmah. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX include the rest of v. 8: The rulers vine of Sibmah in v. 9}</sup> The rulers of the nations have struck down its choicest vines, which once reached Jazer and pushed to the desert. Its shoots spread out and passed over the sea."

<sup>{Isaiah Weeps for Moab}</sup> d "Therefore I weep with the tears of Jazer for the vines of Sibmah. I drench you with my tears, O Heshbon and Elealeh for the shouts of joy over your summer fruit and your grain harvest have ended.

Joy and gladness are taken away from the orchards, in the vineyards people will sing no songs, <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads no songs are sung}</sup> and <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT mss ; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> no cheers are raised. No vintner <sup>{Lit. treader}</sup> treads out wine in the presses, because I've put an end to the shouting.
Therefore my insides <sup>{16:11Or heart ; lit. bowels}</sup> moan like a lyre for Moab, and my innermost being <sup>{Or my very soul}</sup> for Kir-hareseth.
When Moab appears, when he arrives <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read tires himself}</sup> upon the high place and comes to his sanctuary to pray, he will not prevail."
This was the message that the Lord spoke concerning Moab in the past.

But now the Lord has spoken again: "Within three years, like the years of a contract worker, <sup>{I.e. as if carefully counted pursuant to an employment contract; cf. Isa 21:16}</sup> Moab's glory will be brought into contempt, in spite of all its great multitude, and its survivors will be very few and <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> of no importance."

<sup>{A Rebuke to Damascus}</sup> d A message <sup>{Lit. An oracle}</sup> about Damascus: <sup>{So MT LXX; 1QIsa a reads Dramascus}</sup> "Look! Damascus <sup>{So MT LXX; 1QIsa a reads Dramascus}</sup> will cease to be a city. Instead, it will become a pile of ruins.

The cities of Oraru <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads Aroer , a pun on the Heb. word for ruins ; LXX reads forever}</sup> will be deserted they will be devoted to herds that will lay at rest, and terrorism will be no more. <sup>{Lit. and no one will make them afraid}</sup>

The fortress will disappear from Ephraim, and royal authority from Damascus; <sup>{So MT LXX; 1QIsa a reads Dramascus}</sup> the survivors <sup>{I.e. believing Jews who return}</sup> from Aram <sup>{I.e. Syria}</sup> will be like the glory of the Israelis," declares the Lord of the Heavenly Armies.
<sup>{A Time of Weakness for Israel}</sup> d "At that time, <sup>{Lit. On that day}</sup> Jacob's glory will have become weakened, and his strong <sup>{Lit. fat}</sup> flesh will turn gaunt;

it will be as if harvesters gather standing grain, reaping the ears by hand, <sup>{Lit. ears with his arm}</sup> or it will be as if grain is harvested in the valley of Rephaim. <sup>{Lit. Giants}</sup>

Nevertheless, gleanings will remain in Israel, <sup>{Lit. it}</sup> as when an olive tree is beaten <sup>{Or harvested}</sup> two or three ripe olives left in the topmost branches, four or five left among the branches of a fruit-filled tree," <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads among its branches}</sup> declares the Lord God of Israel.
<sup>{Revival to Come to Israel}</sup> d At that time, men will look upon <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; cf. LXX; MT reads to}</sup> their Maker, and their eyes will honor the Holy One of Israel.

They will not look upon <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; cf. LXX; MT reads to}</sup> the altars, the products <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read the product}</sup> that their own fingers <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read hands}</sup> have made, and they will have no regard for Asherah poles <sup>{I.e. images of the Babylonian-Canaanite goddess of fortune}</sup> or incense altars. <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; LXX reads for trees or for their abominations}</sup>

<sup>{Desolation to the Nations}</sup> d "At that time, <sup>{Lit. On that day,}</sup> their fortified cities that they abandoned because of the Israelis will be like desolate places <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; cf. LXX; MT reads place}</sup> of the forests and hilltops <sup>{Or the Hivites and Amorites}</sup> there will be desolation.
For you have forgotten the God of your salvation and have not remembered the Rock that is your strength. Therefore even though you plant delightful plants, sowing them with imported vine-seedlings,

at the time that you plant them, carefully making them grow, the very morning you make your seed to sprout, your harvest will be ruined <sup>{Lit. become a pile}</sup> in a time of grief and unbearable pain." <sup>{Lit. and sorrow}</sup>
"How terrible it will be for many peoples, who rage like the roaring sea! Oh, how the uproar of nations is like the sound of rushing, mighty water How they roar!

The nations roar like the rushing of many waters, <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; cf. LXX; MT ms Syr lack this line}</sup> but the Lord <sup>{Lit. but he}</sup> will rebuke them, and they will run far away, chased like chaff blown down from the mountains or like thick dust <sup>{Lit. like something}</sup> that rolls along, blown along by a wind storm.
When the evening arrives, watch out sudden terror! By morning they will be there no longer! So it will be for those who plunder us and what will happen to those who rob us."
<sup>{A Rebuke to Cush}</sup> d Woe to the land of whirring wings that is beyond the rivers of Cush, <sup>{I.e. Nubia, south of Egypt (modern northern Sudan)}</sup>

which sends envoys by the sea, <sup>{Or Nile}</sup> in papyrus boats over the water! Go, swift messengers, to a tall, smooth-skinned nation, to a people feared far and wide, a nation that metes out <sup>{Or nation of strange speech ; so 1QIsa a MT; LXX reads nation without hope}</sup> punishment <sup>{1QIsa a MT LXX lack punishment}</sup> and oppresses, whose land the rivers divide.
All you inhabitants of the world, you who live on the earth, when a banner is raised on the mountains, you'll see it. When a trumpet sounds, you'll hear it!

For this is what the Lord told me: "I will remain quiet and watch in my dwelling place like dazzling heat in sunshine, like a cloud of dew in the heat <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; MT mss LXX read on the day}</sup> of harvest."

For before the harvest, when the season of <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack season of}</sup> budding is over, and sour grapes ripen into mature grapes, <sup>{Lit. flowers}</sup> he cuts off the shoots with pruning knives, clearing away the spreading branches as he lops them off.

And <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT LXX; 4QIsa b lacks And}</sup> they will all be left for birds of prey that live on the mountains <sup>{So 4QIsa b ; 1QIsa a MT read of mountains ; LXX reads mountains of heaven}</sup> and for wild animals. <sup>{Lit. for beasts of the earth ; i.e. non-domesticated animals, as opposed to domesticated livestock;}</sup> Birds of prey will pass the summer feeding on them, and all the wild animals <sup>{Lit. the beasts of the earth ; so 1QIsa a ; i.e. non-domesticated animals, as opposed to domesticated livestock; MT LXX read every beast of the field}</sup> will pass the winter feeding <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack feeding}</sup> on them.

At that time tribute will be brought to the Lord of the Heavenly Armies from <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; 4QIsa b MT lack from}</sup> a tall and smooth-skinned people, from a people feared far and wide, a nation that metes out <sup>{Or nation of strange speech ; so 1QIsa a MT; LXX reads nation with hope}</sup> punishment <sup>{1QIsa a MT LXX lack punishment}</sup> and oppresses, whose land the rivers divide, to Mount Zion, the place that bears <sup>{Lit. place of}</sup> the name of the Lord . <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa b MT LXX read the Lord of the Heavenly Armies}</sup>

<sup>{A Rebuke to Egypt}</sup> d A message <sup>{Lit. An oracle}</sup> about Egypt: "Watch out! The Lord rides on a swift cloud, and is coming to Egypt. The idols of Egypt tremble before him, and the hearts of the Egyptians melt within them.

I will stir up Egyptians against Egyptians, and everyone will fight against his brother, everyone against his neighbor, city <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack and}</sup> against city, kingdom against kingdom.

The spirits of the Egyptians within them will be drained of courage, <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack of courage}</sup> and I will bring their plans to nothing. They will consult idols <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads consult the idols ; LXX reads consult their idols}</sup> and spirits of the dead, and mediums and spiritists.
I will hand the Egyptians over to the power of a cruel master, and a fierce king will rule over them," declares the Lord God of the Heavenly Armies.

<sup>{A Rebuke to Egypt's Ecology and Industry}</sup> d "The water sources <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack sources}</sup> of the Nile <sup>{Or the sea}</sup> will be dried up, and the river <sup>{I.e. the Nile}</sup> will become dry and parched.

The canals will stink, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> the tributaries of Egypt will dwindle and dry up. Reeds and rushes will wither away. <sup>{So 4QIsa b MT; 1QIsa a lacks this line}</sup>

And the bulrushes along the Nile, <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; LXX lacks this line}</sup> along the mouth of the Nile, <sup>{Lit. the River}</sup> will wither away. All the sown fields of the Nile will become parched, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> they will be blown away; there will be nothing left. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa b MT read and will be no more ; LXX lacks this line}</sup>
The fishermen will groan, and all who cast hooks into the Nile will lament; those who spread nets upon the water will become weaker and weaker.
The workers <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa b ; MT LXX read And the workers}</sup> in combed flax and the weavers of white linen will be in despair.
Egypt's <sup>{Lit. Its}</sup> workers in cloth <sup>{Lit. Its weavers}</sup> will be crushed, and all who work for wages will be sick at heart."

<sup>{A Rebuke to Egypt's Leaders}</sup> d Zoan's princes are nothing but fools; the wisest advisors of Pharaoh give stupid advice. How can you say to Pharaoh, "I'm a descendant of wise men, a descendant of ancient kings" ?
Where are your wise men now? Let them tell you, let them make known what the Lord <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa a corrector MT LXX read Lord of the Heavenly Armies}</sup> has planned against Egypt.
The princes of Zoan have become fools, and the princes of Memphis deluded; the leaders <sup>{Or cornerstones}</sup> of its tribes have led Egypt astray.

The Lord has mixed <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT LXX; 4QIsa b reads has poured}</sup> within them <sup>{Lit. it}</sup> a spirit of confusion; so they make Egypt stagger in all that it does, like a drunkard staggers around in his vomit.
