As a result, there will be nothing for Egypt that head or tail, palm branch or reed, can do. <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT LXX; 4QIsa b reads do at that time}</sup>

<sup>{Egypt and Syria Will Worship God}</sup> d At that time, <sup>{Lit. On that day ; so 1QIsa a MT LXX; 4QIsa b lacks At that time}</sup> the Egyptians will be like women they <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa b MT read he}</sup> will shudder and be afraid before the uplifted hand of the Lord of the Heavenly Armies, when he brandishes his hand against her. <sup>{I.e. Egypt; so 1QIsa a ; MT reads Armies, which he brandishes against it ; LXX reads Armies, which he brandishes against them}</sup>

And the land of Judah will become a terror to the Egyptians. Everyone to whom it is mentioned will be afraid, because of the uplifted hand <sup>{Lit. plotting ; so 4QIsa b ; 1QIsa a MT LXX read plan}</sup> of the Lord of the Heavenly Armies that <sup>{So 4QIsa b ; 1QIsa a MT LXX read hand that the Lord of the Heavenly Armies}</sup> is turning in their direction.

At that time, <sup>{Lit. On that day}</sup> there will be five cities in the land of Egypt that speak the language of Canaan and swear allegiance to the Lord of the Heavenly Armies. One of them will be called the City of the Sun. <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa b MT mss ; MT reads Destruction ; LXX reads Asedek City ; i.e. City of Righteousness}</sup>

At that time, <sup>{Lit. On that day}</sup> there will be an altar to the Lord of the Heavenly Armies <sup>{So 4QIsa b ; 1QIsa a MT LXX lack of the Heavenly Armies}</sup> in the heart of the land of Egypt, and a monument to the Lord <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT LXX; 4QIsa b reads Lord of hosts}</sup> at its border.

It will be a sign and a witness to the Lord of the Heavenly Armies in the land of Egypt; when they cry out to the Lord because of their oppressors, he will send them a savior, and he will come down <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read will defend}</sup> and rescue them.

So the Lord will make himself known to the Egyptians, and the Egyptians will acknowledge the Lord . At that time, <sup>{Lit. On that day}</sup> they will worship <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read Lord on that day. And they will worship}</sup> with sacrifices and offerings, and they will make vows to the Lord and carry them out.
The Lord will strike Egypt with a plague, striking but then healing. Then they will turn to the Lord , and he will respond to their pleas and heal them.

At that time, <sup>{Lit. On that day}</sup> there will be a highway from Egypt to Assyria. The Assyrians will come into Egypt, and the Egyptians into Assyria, and they <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa b MT LXX read and the Egyptians}</sup> will worship with the Assyrians.

At that time, <sup>{Lit. On that day}</sup> Israel will be in a triple alliance <sup>{Lit. will be the third}</sup> with Egypt and Assyria; they will be <sup>{DSS MT lack they will be}</sup> a blessing in the midst of the earth.
The Lord of the Heavenly Armies has blessed them, saying, "Blessed be Egypt my people, Assyria the work of my hands, and Israel my inheritance."
<sup>{The Conquest of Egypt and Cush}</sup> d In the year that the supreme commander, sent by Sargon the king of Assyria, came to Ashdod, attacked it, and captured it

at that time the Lord spoke through Amoz's son Isaiah: "Go loosen the sackcloth that's around your waist, <sup>{Lit. your hips and lower back}</sup> and take your sandals <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads sandal}</sup> off your feet." So that's what he did: he went around naked and barefoot.

Then the Lord said, "Just as my servant Isaiah has walked around naked and barefoot for three years as a sign and a warning for Egypt and Ethiopia, <sup>{I.e. Nubia, south of Egypt (modern northern Sudan)}</sup>

so the king of Assyria will lead away the Egyptian captives and exiles from Cush, <sup>{I.e. Nubia, south of Egypt (modern northern Sudan)}</sup> both the young and the old, naked and barefoot with even their buttocks uncovered to the shame <sup>{Or nakedness}</sup> of Egypt.
Then they will be dismayed and put to shame because of Cush, <sup>{I.e. Nubia, south of Egypt (modern northern Sudan)}</sup> their hope, and Egypt, their jewel. <sup>{Or pride}</sup>

At that time, the inhabitants of this coastland will say, «See, this is what has happened to those on whom we counted and relied <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read and to whom we fled}</sup> for help and deliverance from the king of Assyria! How, then, can we escape?»"

<sup>{Elam and Media are Rebuked}</sup> d A message <sup>{Lit. An oracle}</sup> concerning the pasture <sup>{Or plague ; cf. Isa 5:17; 1King 8:37; Jer 14:12; MT LXX read wilderness}</sup> by the Sea. Josh 10:40}</sup> sweep on, it comes from the desert, from a distant <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa a corrector MT LXX read terrible}</sup> land.

Therefore my body is <sup>{Or waist ; lit. my hips and lower back are}</sup> racked with pain; pangs have seized me, like the pangs of a woman in labor; I am so upset that I cannot hear; I am so frightened that I cannot see while I'm reeling around. <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa a ; MT LXX begin v. 4 with this line}</sup>

They set the tables; they spread the carpets; <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; LXX lacks this line}</sup> they eat, they drink! Get up, you officers! Oil the shields!"

When he sees chariots, each man <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa a ; cf. v. 9; MT LXX lack each man}</sup> with a pair of horses, riders on donkeys or riders on <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads train of donkeys or train of}</sup> camels, let him pay attention, full attention."
Then the lookout <sup>{So 1QIsa a Syr; MT reads Then a lion}</sup> shouted: "Upon a watchtower I stand, O Lord, continually by day, and I am stationed at my post throughout the night.

Look! Here come riders, <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads chariot ; cf. v. 7}</sup> each man with a pair of horses!" <sup>{A Message about Arabia}</sup> d A message <sup>{Lit. An oracle}</sup> concerning Arabia. "You will camp in the thickets in Arabia, you caravans of the Dedanites.

And it will come about <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads it came about}</sup> that your choicest valleys will be filled with chariots, and horsemen will take their positions at the gates.
He has uncovered the defenses of Judah." At that time, <sup>{Lit. On that day}</sup> you looked at the arsenal of the Palace of the Forest, <sup>{Cf. 1King 10:16-17}</sup>
and saw that there were many breaches in the City of David. So you stored up water from the Lower Pool,

Tyre}</sup> for desert creatures. <sup>{Or demons}</sup> They raised up her <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads his}</sup> siege towers, they stripped her fortresses bare and turned her into a ruin.

Then, at the end of those 70 years}</sup>
"Take a harp; walk around the city, you forgotten whore! Make sweet melody; sing many songs, and perhaps you'll be remembered."

At the end of 70 years, the Lord will deal with Tyre, at which time she'll return to her courtesan's trade, and prostitute herself with the kingdoms <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read all the kingdoms}</sup> of the world on the surface of the earth.

Nevertheless, her profits and her earnings will be dedicated <sup>{Or given}</sup> to the Lord ; they will not be stored up or hoarded but her profits will go to those who live in the Lord's presence, <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; 4QIsa c reads but will be for those who live in the Lord's presence. And her profits will be}</sup> for abundant food and choice clothing.

<sup>{The Earth is Judged}</sup> d "Watch out! The Lord <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; 4QIsa c reads Lord}</sup> is about to depopulate the land and devastate it; he will turn it upside down <sup>{Or distort its surface}</sup> and scatter its inhabitants.

It will be the same for the lay people as for priests, the same for servants as for their masters, for female servants as for their mistresses, for buyers as for sellers, for lenders as for borrowers, and for creditors as for debtors.
The earth will be utterly depopulated and completely laid waste for the Lord has spoken this message. <sup>{Lit. word}</sup>

"The earth dries up and withers; the world languishes and fades away; heaven fades away, along with the earth. <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa c ; MT reads the heavens fade away ; cf. LXX reads the exalted ones of the earth mourn}</sup>

The earth lies defiled beneath its inhabitants; because they have transgressed the laws, <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; 4QIsa c LXX read the Law}</sup> violated the statutes, and broken the everlasting covenant.

Therefore the curse keeps on consuming, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa c MT LXX read consuming the earth}</sup> and its inhabitants are declared guilty. Furthermore, the inhabitants of earth are ablaze, and few people are left.
The new wine evaporates; the vine and the oil <sup>{So 4QIsa c ; 1QIsa a MT LXX lack and the oil}</sup> dry up; all the merrymakers groan.
"The celebrations of the tambourine have ended, the noise of the jubilant has stopped, and the mirth that the harp produces has ended.

The chaotic city lies broken down; every house is closed up so that no one can enter them. <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack them}</sup>
There is an outcry in the streets over wine; all cheer turns to gloom; the fun times of the earth are banished.

<sup>{Glorifying God}</sup> d "They raise their voices; they shout for joy; <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; 4QIsa c reads and they shout}</sup> from the west <sup>{Lit. sea ; so 1QIsa a MT; cf. <sup>{The Universal Impact of Judgment}</sup> d "Terror and pit and snare are coming in your direction, you inhabitants of the earth!

Therefore strong peoples will glorify you; cities of ruthless nations will revere you.

For you have been a stronghold for the poor, a stronghold for the needy in distress, a shelter from the storm and a shade from the heat for the blistering attack <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack attack}</sup> from the ruthless is like a rainstorm beating against a wall,
and the noise of foreigners is like the heat of the desert. Just as you subdue heat by the shade of clouds, so the victory songs of violent men will be stilled.

<sup>{Celebration of the Righteous}</sup> d "On this mountain, <sup>{I.e. Mount Zion; cf. 24:23}</sup> the Lord of the Heavenly Armies will prepare for all peoples a banquet of rich food, a banquet of well-aged wines rich food full of marrow, and refined wines of the finest <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack the finest}</sup> vintage

he has swallowed up <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT ms ; MT reads And he will swallow up ; cf. LXX Syr Theodotian 1Cor 15:54}</sup> death forever! Then the Lord God will wipe away the tears from all faces, and he will take away the disgrace of his people from the entire earth." for the Lord has spoken.

It's the Lord! <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack It's the Lord}</sup> T his is our God! We waited for him, and he saved us. This is the Lord ! We waited for him, so let us rejoice, and we will <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads and let us}</sup> be glad that he has saved us."

Mount Zion; cf. 24:23}</sup> but the Moabites will be trodden down beneath him, just as straw is trodden down in the slime of <sup>{Lit. in the water of ; so 1QIsa a MT; MT qere reads in ; LXX reads in wagons}</sup> a manure pit.
They will spread out their hands in the thick of it, just as swimmers spread out their hands to swim, but the Lord will bring down their pride, together with the cleverness of their hands.
He brings down the high fortifications of your walls and lays them low; he will raze them <sup>{Or reach ; so 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa c MT read he casts them}</sup> to the ground, right down to the dust.

<sup>{The Song of Redeemed Judah}</sup> d At that time, <sup>{Lit. On that day}</sup> people will sing this song <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b 4QIsa c MT read time, this song will be sung ; LXX reads time, they will sing that song}</sup> in the land of Judah: "We have a strong city; God crafts victory, its walls and ramparts. <sup>{So 4QIsa c ; 1QIsa a MT read walls and ramparts ; LXX reads wall and rampart}</sup>

You will keep perfectly peaceful <sup>{Lit. peace, peace ; so 1QIsa a MT; LXX Syr read peace}</sup> the one whose mind remains focused on you, because he remains <sup>{So 1QIsa a 1QIsa b LXX; 4QIsa c MT read trusts}</sup> in you.
"Trust in the Lord forever, for in the Lord God <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; 4QIsa b reads the Lord God ; LXX reads the Lord, the Lord}</sup> you have an everlasting rock.

For he has made drunk <sup>{So MT 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b 4QIsa b 4QIsa c MT read has brought low ; LXX reads has humbled and brought down}</sup> the inhabitants of the height, the lofty city. He lays it low <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads He levels it, he levels it}</sup> to the ground casting it down to the dust,
by the feet of the oppressed who trample it, <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads The foot tramples it}</sup> by the footsteps of the needy. <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads oppressed}</sup>

"The path of the righteous is level; O Upright One, <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; 4QIsa c reads they go straight ahead ; LXX lacks this line}</sup> you make safe <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read smooth or you prepare}</sup> the way of justice. <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa c ; MT LXX read of the righteous ones}</sup>

Yes, Lord , in the path of your judgments we wait; <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads we wait for you}</sup> your name and your Law <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa c MT read your renown ; cf. LXX}</sup> are the <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; 4QIsa b reads my}</sup> soul's desire.
My soul yearns for you in the night; my spirit within me searches for you. For when your judgments come upon the earth, the world's inhabitants learn righteousness.
If favor is shown to the wicked, they don't learn righteousness; even in a land of uprightness they act perversely and do not perceive the majesty of the Lord .

"Lord , your hand is lifted up, but they do not see it. And <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; cf. LXX; the Heb. lacks And}</sup> let them see your zeal for your <sup>{Lit. the ; so 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks your}</sup> people and be put to shame yes, let the fire reserved for your enemies consume them!
Lord , you will decide <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads prepare or give ; LXX reads Lord , give}</sup> peace for us, for you have indeed accomplished all our achievements for us.

The dead won't live, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; 1QIsa a corrector MT lack and}</sup> the departed spirits won't rise to that end, you punished and destroyed them, then locked away <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read wiped out}</sup> all memory of them.

"But you have enlarged the nation, <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa b}</sup> Lord ; you have enlarged the nation. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT ms lacks this line}</sup> You have gained honor; you have extended all the borders of the land.

Lord , they <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; MT mss LXX mss read we; LXX reads I remembered you}</sup> came to you in distress; they poured out their secret <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa b MT read out a magical}</sup> prayer when your chastenings were <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read your chastening was}</sup> afflicting <sup>{Lit. upon}</sup> them.
Just as a pregnant woman writhes and cries out during her labor when she is about to give birth, so were we because of you, Lord .
We were pregnant, writhing in pain, but we gave birth only to wind. We have not won your <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT lacks your}</sup> victory on earth, nor have the inhabitants of the world been born."

<sup>{Israel's Deliverance}</sup> d At that time, <sup>{Lit. On that day}</sup> with his fierce, mighty, and powerful sword, the Lord will punish the gliding serpent Leviathan the coiling serpent Leviathan and he will kill the dragon that's in the sea.
At that time, <sup>{Lit. On that day}</sup> "A fermenting <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read pleasant}</sup> vineyard sing about it!
I, the Lord , watch over it And I water it continually. I guard it night and day so no one can harm it.

I am not angry. If only the vineyard <sup>{Lit. only it}</sup> could give me briers and thorns <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads thorns}</sup> to battle, I would march against it, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> I would burn it all up.
Or else let it lay claim to my protection; let it make peace with me, yes, let it make peace with me."
In times to come, Jacob will take root, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> Israel will blossom, sprout shoots, and fill the whole world with fruit.
Has the Lord <sup>{Lit. Has he}</sup> struck them down, just as he struck down those who struck them? Or have they been killed, just as their killers were killed?
Measure by measure, <sup>{Or With war cries}</sup> in their exile you contended with them; with his fierce blast he removed them, as on a day when the east wind blows.

By this, then, Jacob's guilt will be atoned for, and this will be the full harvest that comes from the removal of his sin: when he makes all the altar stones like pulverized chalkstones, no Asherah <sup>{Or sacred}</sup> poles or incense altars will be left standing.
For the fortified city stands desolate, a settlement abandoned and forsaken like the desert; calves graze there, and there they lie down and strip bare its branches.

When its branches are dry, they are broken off, and women come and kindle fires with them, since this is a people who show no consideration. That is why the One who made them shows them no compassion; the One who created them shows them no mercy.

<sup>{Assyria and Egypt Exiles Redeemed}</sup> d At that time, <sup>{Lit. On that day}</sup> the Lord will winnow grain from the Euphrates River <sup>{DSS MT lack River}</sup> channel to the Wadi <sup>{I.e. a seasonal stream or river that channels water during rain seasons but is dry at other times}</sup> of Egypt, <sup>{I.e. the southwestern-most border of ancient Philistia}</sup> and you will be gathered in one by one, O people of Israel.

Furthermore, at that time, <sup>{Lit. Furthermore, on that day}</sup> a great trumpet will be sounded, and those who were perishing in the land of Assyria and those who had been expelled <sup>{Or exiled}</sup> to the land of Egypt will come and worship the Lord on his holy mountain at Jerusalem.

<sup>{The Captivity of Ephraim}</sup> d How terrible it will be for that arrogant garland the drunks of Ephraim! How terrible it will be for that fading flower of his glorious beauty, which sits on the heads of people bloated with food, <sup>{Lit. the valley of those grown fat}</sup> of people overcome with wine!

Look! The Lord <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads Lord}</sup> has one who is mighty and strong, like a hailstorm and destructive tempest, like a storm of mighty, overflowing water and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> he will give rest to the land.

With hands <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; cf. LXX; or He will throw them forcefully down to the ground ; cf. MT}</sup> and feet, that proud garland <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; or 3 Underfoot that proud garland ; cf. MT}</sup> those drunks of Ephraim will be trampled.

And that fading flower, his glorious beauty, which sits on the heads of people bloated with food, will be like an early fig before summer whenever someone sees it, he swallows it as soon as it's in his hand.
At that time, <sup>{Lit. On that day}</sup> the Lord of the Heavenly Armies will become a glorious crown, a beautiful diadem for the remnant of his people,
and a spirit of justice to the one who sits in judgment, a source of strength to those who turn back the battle at the gate.

These people also <sup>{1QIsa a lacks people also ; MT lacks people}</sup> stagger from wine and reel from strong drink. Priests and prophets stagger from strong drink; they're drunk from <sup>{Lit. are devoured by}</sup> wine; they reel from strong drink, waver when seeing visions, and stumble when rendering decisions.
For all the tables are covered in vomit and filth, with no clean <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack clean}</sup> space left.
<sup>{Misuse of God's Word}</sup> d To whom will he teach knowledge, and to whom will he explain the message? To children just weaned from milk? To those just taken from the breast?
For it is: "Do this and do that, do this and do that, Line upon line, line upon line, a little here, a little there."
Very well, then, through the mouths of foreigners <sup>{Or through foreign lips}</sup> and foreign languages the Lord will speak to this people
to whom he said, "This is the resting place, so give rest to the weary"'and, "This is the place of repose" but they would not listen.

So, then, the message from the Lord to them will become: "Do this and do that, do this and do that, line upon line, line upon line, a little here, a little there,"so that they will go, but fall backward, and be injured, snared, and captured.
<sup>{God's Precious Cornerstone}</sup> d "Therefore hear <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa c MT LXX read hear (pl.)}</sup> the message from the Lord , you scoffers who rule this people that are in Jerusalem.

Because you said: «We have entered into a covenant with death, and we have an agreement with Sheol, <sup>{I.e. the place where the dead dwell in the afterlife}</sup> so when the overwhelming scourge makes its choice, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read sweeps by}</sup> it cannot reach us, since we have made lies our refuge and have concealed ourselves inside falsehood'

therefore this is what the Lord God <sup>{So 1QIsa a corrector}</sup> says: "Look! I am laying <sup>{So 1QIsa a 1QIsa b ; MT reads I have laid}</sup> a foundation stone in Zion, a tested stone, a precious cornerstone for a sure <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; MT mss LXX lack sure}</sup> foundation: Whoever believes firmly will not act hastily.
And I will make justice the measuring line, and righteousness the plumb line; hail will sweep away your refuge of lies, and floods <sup>{Lit. waters}</sup> will overflow your hiding place.

"Then your covenant with death will be annulled, and your agreement with Sheol <sup>{I.e. the place where the dead dwell in the afterlife}</sup> will not stand; when the overwhelming scourge sweeps by, you will be trampled by it.

As often as it sweeps through, it will carry you away, for it will sweep by morning after morning in the day; but understanding this message will bring sheer terror at night, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads by day and by night; and there will be sheer terror.}</sup>
because the bed is too short to stretch out on, and its blankets too narrow to wrap around oneself!

<sup>{God is on Mount Perazim}</sup> d For the Lord will stand upon <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads as}</sup> Mount Perazim, <sup>{I.e. a mountain near Jerusalem, perhaps the Mount of Olives; cf. 2Sam 6:8}</sup> he will rouse himself in <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads as}</sup> the Valley of Gibeon; to carry out his work his strange deed, and to perform his task his alien task!

But as for you, <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads So now}</sup> don't start mocking, or your chains will become tighter; for I have heard from the Lord <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT mss LXX Syr; MT reads the Lord God}</sup> of the Heavenly Armies about destruction, and it is decreed against the whole land.
<sup>{The God who Plows and Harvests}</sup> d "Pay attention! Listen to what I have to say; Pay attention, and hear my speech.
Does he who plows for sowing plow all the time? Does he keep on breaking up and harrowing his field?

When he has leveled its surface, he scatters caraway <sup>{Or scatters black cumin}</sup> and sows cumin, doesn't he? He plants wheat in rows, barley in its designated place, and feed for livestock <sup>{Lit. and spelt ; i.e. a grass grown and used as fodder}</sup> around its borders, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads its border ; LXX reads your borders}</sup> doesn't he?
His God instructs him regarding the correct way, directing him how to plant. <sup>{DSS MT lack how to plant}</sup>
For caraway is not threshed with a sharp sledge, nor is a cart wheel rolled over cumin. Instead, caraway is winnowed with a stick, and cumin with a rod.

It <sup>{I.e. grain ; so 1QIsa a MT; 4QIsa k reads And it}</sup> must be ground; <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa k MT read must be ground for bread}</sup> one cannot keep threshing it forever. Even if he drives his cart <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa a corrector MT read the wheel of his cart}</sup> and horses over it, he cannot crush it.

This insight also comes from the Lord of the Heavenly Armies, who is distinguished <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads wonderful}</sup> in practical advice and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> magnificent in sound wisdom."

<sup>{Judgment is Coming to Jerusalem}</sup> d "How terrible it will be for you, Aruel, Aruel, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read Ariel, Ariel ; i.e. a nickname assigned by the prophet for Jerusalem}</sup> the city where David encamped! Year after year, let your festivals run their cycle.

Then I'll besiege Aruel, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read Ariel, Ariel ; i.e. an allusion to Jerusalem}</sup> and there will be sorrow and mourning; she will become to me like an altar fireplace. <sup>{Lit. an Ariel ; i.e. perhaps a pun on the name Aruel}</sup>
Then I'll encamp against you like David, <sup>{So 4QIsa k MT mss LXX; 1QIsa a MT read you on all sides}</sup> and I'll lay siege to you with towers, raise siege works against you,
and you will be brought down. You will speak from the ground, and your speech will mumble from the dust. Your voice will come ghostlike from the ground, and your speech will whisper from the dust.

"But the hordes of your enemies <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads foreigners ; LXX reads the ungodly}</sup> will become like fine dust, and the hordes of tyrants like flying chaff. Then suddenly, in an instant,
you will be visited by the Lord of the Heavenly Armies with thunder, an earthquake, and great noise, with a windstorm, a tempest, and flames from a devouring fire.

Then the hordes of all the nations that fight against Aruel, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read Ariel, Ariel ; i.e. an allusion to Jerusalem}</sup> all that attack her and her fortification <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads her mountain stronghold ; LXX reads Jerusalem}</sup> and besiege her, will become like a dream, with its visions in the night

as when a hungry man dreams he eats, but wakes up still hungry; or when a thirsty man dreams he drinks, but wakes up faint, with his thirst unquenched. So will it be with the hordes of all the nations that fight against Mount Zion.

<sup>{Blind to God's Words}</sup> d "Act stupid! Be astonished! Act blind, and be blind! Be drunk, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads They have become drunk}</sup> but not from <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT lacks from}</sup> wine; stagger around, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads They stagger around}</sup> but not from strong drink.
For the Lord has poured out upon you a spirit of deep sleep he has closed your eyes, you prophets, he has covered your heads, you seers!"

"And this entire vision has become for you like the words of a sealed book. When people give it to someone who can read, and say, «Read this, please,'he answers, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read he will answer}</sup> «I cannot, because it is sealed.'

Or when they give the book <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read the book will be given}</sup> to someone who cannot read, and say, «Read this, please,'he answers, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read he will answer}</sup> «I don't know how to read.»"

<sup>{A Rebuke of Hypocritical Worship}</sup> d Then the Lord said: "Because these people draw near with their mouths and honor me with their lips, but their hearts are far from me, worship of me <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads Their worship of me}</sup> has become merely like <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack like}</sup> rules taught by human beings.

Therefore, watch out! "As for me, <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; the Heb. lacks as for me}</sup> I will once again do amazing things with this people, wonder upon wonder. The wisdom of their wise men will perish, and the insights <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read insight}</sup> of their discerning men will stay hidden."

<sup>{A Rebuke to the Deceptive}</sup> d "How terrible it will be for you who go to great depths to hide your plans from the Lord , you whose deeds have been <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read deeds are (or will be )}</sup> done <sup>{DSS MT lack done}</sup> in the dark, and who say, «Who can see us? Who has recognized <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read recognizes}</sup> us? '

He has turned the tables on you <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads You turn things upside down!}</sup> as if the potter were thought to be like heat. <sup>{I.e. the fire in a kiln; so 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read clay}</sup> Can what is made say of the one who made it, «He did not make me? 'Or can what is formed say of the ones <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read one}</sup> who formed it, «He has no skill? '
"In a very little while, will not Lebanon be turned into a garden of fruit, <sup>{Lit. into Carmel}</sup> and the garden of fruit <sup>{Lit. and Carmel}</sup> seem like a forest?
On that day the deaf will hear the words of a scroll, and out of gloom and darkness the eyes of the blind will see.
The humble will again experience joy in the Lord , and the poorest people will rejoice in the Holy One of Israel.
For the ruthless will vanish, and mockers will disappear, and all who have an eye for evil will be cut down

those who make a person appear to be the offender in a lawsuit, who set a trap for someone who is making his defense in court, <sup>{Lit. in the gate}</sup> and push aside the innocent with specious arguments.
"Therefore, this is what the Lord , who redeemed Abraham, says concerning the house of Jacob: «No longer will Jacob be ashamed; no longer will his face grow pale.
For when he sees in his midst his children, the work of my hands, they will keep my name holy; they will sanctify the Holy One of Jacob and stand in awe of the God of Israel.
Moreover, those who go astray in spirit will gain <sup>{Lit. discover}</sup> understanding, and those who complain will accept instruction.»"
<sup>{Foolish Trust in Egypt}</sup> d "Oh, you stubborn children,"declares the Lord , "who carry out plans but they are not mine, and who make alliances but not by my Spirit, piling sin upon sin.
They set out to go down to Egypt, without asking my advice; taking refuge in Pharaoh's protection, and seeking shelter in Egypt's shadow.
But Pharaoh's protection will become your shame, and sheltering in Egypt's shadow your longing. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read disgrace}</sup>
And it will turn out that <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read For even though}</sup> his officials are at Zoan, and his envoys will reach Hanes.

There is only loathsome destruction <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads Everyone comes to shame}</sup> through a people that cannot benefit them, who bring neither help nor profit, but only shame and disgrace."

<sup>{The Animals of the Negev}</sup> d An oracle about the animals of the Negev: <sup>{I.e. southern regions of the Sinai peninsula; cf. Josh 10:40}</sup> "Through a land of trouble, dryness, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack dryness ; cf. Isa 41:18}</sup> and distress, of lionesses and roaring lions, where there is no water, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read from whence come}</sup> a land of vipers and darting snakes, he carries <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read they carry}</sup> their riches on donkeys'backs, and their treasures on the humps of camels, to a nation that cannot benefit them,

to Egypt, which gives help that is worthless and useless. Therefore I call her, «Rahab, <sup>{The Heb. word Rahab means The One who Storms ; i.e. Egypt; cf. Isa 51:9; Ps 87:4}</sup> who just sits still.»"

<sup>{The Illusions of False Prophecy}</sup> d "Go now, and write it down <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; 4QIsa c LXX read write down}</sup> on a tablet in their presence, inscribing it in a book, so that for times to come it may be an everlasting witness.
For they are a rebellious people, deceitful children, children unwilling to hear the Lord's instruction.
They say to the seers, «Don't see visions,'and to the prophets, «Don't give us visions of what is right! Instead, tell us welcome things, prophesy illusions,
get out of the way, turn aside from the path, and stop confronting us with the Holy One of Israel." <sup>{Lit. bring to an end the Holy One from before us .}</sup>

<sup>{Rejecting God's Message}</sup> d Therefore, this is what the Holy One of Israel says: "Because you reject this message, and put your trust in oppression and enjoy it, <sup>{Apparent meaning 1QIsa a ; MT reads and are perverse}</sup> and since you rely on it,
therefore, for you this sin will become like a breach in a high wall that is about to collapse, bulging out, and whose crash comes suddenly in an instant.

Its breaking will be like when potters'vessels are broken, shattered so ruthlessly <sup>{Lit. broken they do not take pity ; so 1QIsa a ; MT reads broken he does not take pity}</sup> that among its fragments not even a broken sliver will be found for taking fire from a hearth or scooping water out of a cistern."

For this is what the Lord <sup>{So 1QIsa a}</sup> God , <sup>{So 1QIsa a corrector}</sup> the Holy One of Israel, says: "In repentance and rest you will be saved; in staying calm and trusting will be your strength. But you refused.
Instead, you said, «No! We'll escape on horses!'Therefore, you'll flee away. And you said, «We'll ride off on swift steeds!'Therefore your pursuers will be swift.

A thousand will flee at the threat of one; and run away, pursued by <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read away at the threat of}</sup> five, until you are left like a flagpole on a mountaintop, <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads the mountaintop}</sup> like a banner on a hill."

<sup>{Restoration is Promised to Israel}</sup> d "Nevertheless, the Lord will wait so he can be gracious to you; and thus he will rise up to show you mercy. For the Lord is a God of justice. How blessed are all those who wait for him."

Indeed, you people who live in Zion and in Jerusalem, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; cf. LXX; MT reads at Jerusalem}</sup> you <sup>{So 1QIsa a (pl.); MT (sing.)}</sup> will weep no more. How gracious the Lord <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read he}</sup> will be to you at the sound of your cry! As soon as he hears it, he will answer you.

And although the Lord gives you the bread of adversity and the water <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT lacks the correct Heb. construct}</sup> of affliction, your teachers won't hide themselves <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads himself}</sup> anymore, but your own eyes will see your teachers.
And whether you turn to the right or turn to the left, your ears will hear a message behind you: "This is the way, walk in it."

Then you will defile your carved idols that are overlaid with silver and your images plated with gold. You'll throw them away like disgusting objects <sup>{Lit. like menstrual rags}</sup> and say to them, "Away with you!"

He will also provide rain for your seed that you sow in the ground, and the food that comes from the ground will be <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads and it will be}</sup> rich and abundant. At that time, <sup>{Lit. On that day}</sup> your cattle will graze in broad meadows,
and oxen and donkeys that work the ground will eat seasoned <sup>{Lit. salted}</sup> fodder that workers will winnow with shovels and forks.
And on every lofty mountain and every high hill there will be brooks and canals <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads streams}</sup> running with water on the day of the great slaughter, when the towers fall.

Moreover, the light of the moon will be like the light of the sun, and the sun's light will be seven times brighter, like the light of seven full days, <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; LXX lacks like the light of seven full days}</sup> when the Lord binds up the bruises of his people and heals the wounds inflicted by his blow.
See, the name of the Lord comes from far away, burning with his anger, and in thick rising smoke; his lips are full of fury, and his tongue is like a devouring fire.

His breath is like an overflowing torrent, and it rises right up to the neck, to shake <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads to sift ; LXX reads to confuse}</sup> the nations in the sieve of destruction, and to place in the jaws of the peoples a bit that leads them astray.

You will have songs as on nights when people celebrate a holy festival, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads one celebrates a holy festival}</sup> and gladness of heart, as when they set out with flutes to go to the Lord's mountain, to the Rock of Israel.

<sup>{God's Judgment on Assyria}</sup> d And the Lord will make heard yes, he will make heard <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read heard only once}</sup> his majestic voice, and make his arm <sup>{I.e. the Messiah}</sup> seen descending in raging anger and in a flame of consuming fire, with a cloudburst, thunderstorm and hailstones.
Indeed, the Assyrians will be shattered at the Lord's voice, when he strikes them with his scepter.

And every stroke of his punishing rod <sup>{So MT mss ; 1QIsa a reads the rod of his foundation ; MT reads the rod of foundation}</sup> that the Lord brings down on them will be to the sound of tambourines and harps, as he fights against her <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; MT qere, mss read against them}</sup> in battle with a brandished arm.

For the Fire Pit <sup>{Lit. the Topheth ; i.e. a fire pit near Jerusalem where the Canaanite deity Molech was worshipped}</sup> has long been prepared; truly it is for the king; it will indeed be made ready. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads it is made ready for the king ; cf. LXX}</sup> And <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT lacks And}</sup> its pyre will be deep and wide, with abundant fire and wood. Like a stream of burning sulfur, the breath of the Lord will set it ablaze.

<sup>{Only the Lord can Help}</sup> d "How terrible it will be for those who go down to <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; the Heb. lacks to}</sup> Egypt for help, who rely on horses, who trust in the chariot, because there are so many, and in charioteers, <sup>{Or horsemen}</sup> because they are so strong but do not look to <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read upon}</sup> the Holy One of Israel or seek the Lord !
Yet he is also wise and can bring disaster; he does not take back his words, but will rise up against the house of those who practice evil and against anyone who assists people who work iniquity.

The Egyptians are men, not God, and their horses are physical, <sup>{Or flesh}</sup> not spirit. When the Lord stretches out his hand, anyone who assists will stumble, and the one who is helped will fall; and they will all perish together."

<sup>{The Lord will Defend Jerusalem}</sup> d For this is what the Lord told me: "Just as a lion or a young lion growls over his objects of prey, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read his prey (sing.)}</sup> even when a whole band of shepherds is called out against it, it is not alarmed at their shouting or disturbed by their clamor so the Lord of the Heavenly Armies will come down to do battle on Mount Zion and on its hill.

Like birds hovering overhead, so the Lord of the Heavenly Armies will protect Jerusalem; he will shield and deliver it; and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; cf. LXX; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> he will pass over <sup>{I.e. as the Angel of Death passed over the Israelis; cf. Exod 12:13, 23, 27}</sup> and bring it to safety. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads rescue it}</sup>
Turn back to him, yes to him whom <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads back to him whom}</sup> your people <sup>{Lit. whom they}</sup> have so greatly betrayed, you people of Israel.

For at that time, <sup>{Lit. on that day}</sup> everyone will throw away their <sup>{Lit. his}</sup> idols of silver and their <sup>{Lit. his}</sup> idols of gold that your hands have sinfully made for yourselves.

"Then Assyria will fall by a sword that is not from human beings only <sup>{Lit. not of man}</sup> a sword not wielded by mortal beings will devour them. They will flee from the sword, and their young men will be put to forced labor.

Their stronghold will vanish by reason of terror, and their commanders will be filled with alarm because of the battle standard," declares the Lord , whose fire is in Zion and whose furnace is in Jerusalem.
<sup>{The Government of Justice}</sup> d "Look, a king will reign in righteousness, and rulers will rule with justice.

Each one will be like a shelter from the wind and a hiding place from <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads of}</sup> storms, like streams of water in the desert, in <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads like}</sup> the shadow of a great rock in an exhausted <sup>{Or thirsty}</sup> land.
Then the eyes of those who can see won't turn away, and the ears of those who can hear will listen.
The hearts of reckless people will understand sound judgment, and the tongues of those who stammer will be ready to speak clearly.
People will no longer call a fool <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads No longer will a fool will be called}</sup> noble, nor will a bad person be declared honorable.

For fools utter contempt, and their minds plot <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads work}</sup> wrong things: practicing ungodliness, spreading lies about the Lord , leaving the pangs of hungry people unsatisfied, and depriving thirsty people of drink.

Furthermore, the crimes of bad people are evil; and <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> they devise wicked schemes, destroying the poor <sup>{1QIsa a and MT use two different synonyms}</sup> with lying words, even when needy people plead <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; cf. LXX; MT reads a needy one pleads}</sup> a just cause.
But those who are decent plan noble things, and by noble deeds they stand."
<sup>{A Rebuke for Complacent Women}</sup> d "As for you ladies of leisure Get up and listen to my voice! You daughters who feel so complacent hear what I have to say!
In little more than a year, you complacent women will shudder; for the grape harvest will fail, and the fruit harvest will not <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads without}</sup> come.

So tremble, you ladies of leisure! Shudder, you daughters who feel so complacent! Strip down and make yourselves naked down to the waist! <sup>{Lit. the loins ; so 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads to loins}</sup> Then wrap yourself in <sup>{1QIsa a MT LXX lack yourself in}</sup> sackcloth and beat your breasts. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack and beat your breasts}</sup>
For people will be beating their breasts in mourning <sup>{1QIsa a MT lack mourning}</sup> over the pleasant fields, over the fruitful vines,
and over the land of my people overgrown with thorns and <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> briers yes, over all the houses of merriment and over this city of revelry.

"For the palace will be abandoned, the noisy city deserted; the citadel and watchtower will become barren wastes forever, the delight of wild donkeys, and a pasture for <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads of ; cf. LXX}</sup> flocks,
until the Spirit from on high is poured upon us, and the desert becomes a fertile field, and the fertile field seems like a forest."
<sup>{Restoration of God's Reign}</sup> d "Then justice will live in the wilderness, and righteousness will dwell in the fertile field.
The effect of righteousness will be peace, and the result of righteousness will be quietness and confidence forever.
My people will live in peaceful dwellings, in secure homes and in undisturbed resting places.
But it will hail when the forest comes down, and the wood <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads the city ; LXX lacks the wood}</sup> will be leveled completely.
How happy you will be, sowing your seed beside every stream, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> letting your <sup>{Lit. letting the feet of your}</sup> cattle and donkeys range freely!"

<sup>{God's Judgment}</sup> d "How terrible it will be for you, destroyer, you who have not been destroyed yourself! And how terrible it will be for you, traitor, one whom <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; MT mss read you whom}</sup> people have not betrayed! When you have sunk so low in <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads stopped}</sup> destroying others, you will be destroyed; and when you have finished betraying, they will betray you."

<sup>{A Prayer for Grace}</sup> d "Lord , be gracious to us; we long for you; and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> be our strength <sup>{Lit. arm}</sup> every morning, our salvation in times of trouble.
At the thunder of your voice, the peoples flee; at your silence, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads when you rise up ; LXX reads from fear of you}</sup> the nations scatter.

Your plunder is gathered as when grasshoppers gather; just like <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks just like}</sup> locusts pounce, people have pounced <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads people pounce}</sup> on it.
"The Lord is exalted, for he lives on high; he has filled Zion with justice and righteousness.
He will be a sure foundation for your times, abundance and salvation, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads of salvation}</sup> wisdom and knowledge the fear of the Lord is Zion's treasure."
<sup>{Israel's Unenviable Plight}</sup> d "Listen! Their brave men cry out in the streets; the envoys of peace weep bitterly.

The highways are deserted; travelers have quit the road. The enemy <sup>{Lit. He}</sup> has broken treaties; he despises their witnesses, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads cities}</sup> and respects no one.
The land mourns and wastes away; Lebanon feels ashamed and withers. Sharon is like a desert; Bashan <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads and Bashan}</sup> and Carmel shake off their leaves."
<sup>{God is Exalted}</sup> d "Now I'll rise up,"the Lord has said, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read says the Lord}</sup> "now I'll exalt myself; now I'll be lifted up.
You conceive dried grass, you give birth to stubble; your breath is a fire that will consume you.
And the peoples will be burned as if to ashes; like cut thorn bushes, they will be set ablaze.

"Those who are far away have heard <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; cf. LXX; MT reads You who are far away, hear}</sup> what I've done; and those that are near have acknowledged <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads you that are near, acknowledge}</sup> my power.
The sinners in Zion are terrified; trembling grips the godless: "Who among us can live with the consuming fire? Who among us can live with everlasting flames?"

The one who walks righteously and has spoken <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read and who speaks}</sup> sincere words, who rejects gain from extortion and waves his hand, rejecting bribes, who blocks his ears from hearing plots of murder and shuts his eyes against seeing evil
this is the one who will live on the heights; his refuge will be a mountain fortress. His food will be supplied, and his water will be guaranteed.
"Your eyes will see the king in his elegance, and will view a land that stretches afar.
Your mind will ponder at that time of terror: «Where is the king's accountant? Where is the one who weighed the revenue? Where is the officer who supervises <sup>{Lit. counts}</sup> the towers? '
No longer will you <sup>{So 1QIsa a (pl.); MT (sing.)}</sup> see those arrogant people, those people with their obscure speech you cannot comprehend, stammering in a language you cannot understand.

"Look at Zion, city of our festivals! <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT mss ; MT reads our festival}</sup> Your eyes will see Jerusalem, an undisturbed abode, an immovable tent; its stakes will never be pulled up, nor will any of its ropes be broken.
But there the Lord in majesty will be for us our source <sup>{Lit. us a place}</sup> of broad rivers and streams, where no galley with oars can go, where no stately ship can sail.
For the Lord is our judge, and the Lord is our lawgiver; and the Lord is our king, and it is he who will save us.

"Speak tenderly to Jerusalem, and proclaim to her that her heavy service has been completed, that her penalty has been paid, that she has received from the Lord's hand double for all her sins."
A voice cries out: «In the wilderness prepare the way for the Lord ; and <sup>{So1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack and}</sup> in the desert a straight highway for our God.'
Every valley will be lifted up, and every mountain and hill will be lowered; the rough ground will become level, and the mountain ridges made a plain.
Then the glory of the Lord will be revealed, and all humanity will see it at once; for the mouth of the Lord has spoken."

<sup>{The Word of God Endures Forever}</sup> d A voice says, "Cry out!" So I <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads he}</sup> asked, "What am I to cry out?" "All humanity is grass, and all its loyalty <sup>{Or glory}</sup> is like the flowers of the field.
Grass withers and flowers fade away when the Lord's breath blows on them; surely the people are like grass. <sup>{So MT; 1QIsa a LXX lack this v.}</sup>
Grass withers and flowers fade away, when the Lord's breath blows on them, but the word of <sup>{So MT LXX; 1QIsa a lacks the word of}</sup> our God will stand forever. "

<sup>{Here is Your God}</sup> d "Climb up a high mountain, you messenger of good news to Zion! Lift up your voice with strength, you messenger to Jerusalem! Lift it up! Don't be afraid! Say to the towns of Judah, «Here is your God!'
Look! The Lord G od comes with strength, and his arm <sup>{I.e. the Messiah}</sup> rules for him. Look! His reward is with him, and his payment accompanies him.
Like a shepherd, he tends his flock. He gathers the lambs in his arms, carries them close to his heart, and gently leads the mother sheep."

<sup>{Who is Like the Lord ?}</sup> d "Who has measured the waters of the sea <sup>{Lit. from}</sup> in the hollow of his hand and marked off the heavens by the width of his hand? <sup>{So 1QIsa a Syr; MT LXX read by a hand's width}</sup> Who has enclosed the dust of the earth in a measuring bowl, or weighed the mountains in scales and the hills in a balance?
Who has fathomed the Spirit of the Lord , or as his counselor has taught him? <sup>{I.e. the Spirit; so 1QIsa a ; MT reads the Lord}</sup>
With whom did he consult to enlighten and instruct him on the path of justice? Or who taught him knowledge and showed him the way of wisdom?
"Look! The nations are like a drop in a bucket, and are reckoned as dust on the scales. Look! He even lifts up the islands like powder!
Lebanon would not provide enough fuel, nor are its animals enough for a burnt offering. <sup>{40:14b-16 So MT LXX; 1QIsa a includes these lines by a later scribe}</sup>
All the nations are as nothing before him they are reckoned by him as <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads as less than}</sup> nothing and chaos.

"To whom, then, will you compare me, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack me}</sup> the One who is <sup>{1QIsa a LXX MT lack the One who is}</sup> God? Or to what image will you liken me? <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read him}</sup>
To an idol? A craftsman makes <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads casts}</sup> the image, and a goldsmith overlays it with gold and casts silver chains.

To the impoverished person? He prepares <sup>{1QIsa a MT LXX lack prepares}</sup> an offering <sup>{So MT; later 1QIsa a scribe includes this line}</sup> wood that won't rot Or to the one who chooses a skilled craftsman <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read chooses wood}</sup> and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT lacks and}</sup> seeks <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads seeks a skilled craftsman}</sup> to erect an idol that won't topple?"

<sup>{The Majesty of the Lord}</sup> d "You know, don't you? You have heard, haven't you? Hasn't it been told you from the beginning? Haven't you understood from the foundations of the <sup>{So MT; implied in 1QIsa a}</sup> earth?
He's the one who sits above the disk of the earth, and its inhabitants are like grasshoppers. He's the one who stretches out the heavens like a curtain, and spreads them like a tent to live in,
who brings princes to nothing, and makes void the rulers of the earth.

No sooner are they planted, no sooner are they sown, no sooner have <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa b MT LXX read has}</sup> their stems taken root in the earth, than <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa b MT read and then ; LXX lacks than}</sup> he blows on them, and they wither, and the tempest sweeps them away like stubble.
"To <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa b MT read And to}</sup> whom, then, will you compare me, and to whom should I be equal?" asks the Holy One.

"Lift your eyes up to heaven and see who created all these the one who leads out their vast array of stars by number, calling them all by name because of his great might and his <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack his}</sup> powerful strength <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads strong}</sup> and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack and}</sup> not one is missing."
<sup>{The Lord Watches Israel}</sup> d "Jacob, why do you say and Israel, why do you complain «My predicament is hidden from the Lord , and my cause is ignored by my God.'?

Don't you know? Haven't you heard? The Lord is the eternal God, the Creator of the ends of the earth. He does not grow tired or weary; and <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> his understanding cannot be fathomed.
He's the <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks The}</sup> one who gives might to the faint, renewing strength for the powerless.
Even boys grow tired and weary, and young men collapse and fall,

but those who keep waiting for the Lord will renew their strength. Then <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack Then}</sup> they'll soar on wings like eagles; they'll run and not grow weary; they'll walk and not grow tired."
<sup>{The Lord Comes as Judge}</sup> d "Be silent before me, you coastlands, and let the people renew their strength! Let them come forward, then let them speak together let's draw near for a ruling.

Who has aroused victory from the east, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack and}</sup> has summoned it to his service, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> has handed over nations to him? Who brings down kings, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> turns them into dust with his sword, into windblown stubble with his bow?

And <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> who pursues them and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack and}</sup> moves on unscathed by a path that his feet don't know? <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads travel}</sup>
Who has performed and carried this out, calling the generations from the beginning? I, the Lord the first and will be with the last I am the One!"

<sup>{Idolators Encourage Each Other}</sup> d "The coastlands have looked and are afraid; the ends of the earth have drawn near together <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads earth tremble}</sup> and come forward.
Each helps his neighbor, saying <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT read and saying ; cf. LXX}</sup> to each other, «Be strong!'

The craftsman encourages the goldsmith, and the hammersmith <sup>{Lit. he who smoothes with the hammer}</sup> encourages the one who strikes the anvil. He says <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads saying}</sup> about the welding, «It's good!'and he reinforces it with nails so that it won't topple."
<sup>{The Lord Encourages Israel}</sup> d "But as for you, Israel, my servant, Jacob, whom I've chosen, the offspring of my friend Abraham
you whom I encouraged from the ends of the earth and called from its farthest corners, and told you, «You're my servant, I've chosen you and haven't cast you aside.'
Don't be afraid, because I'm with you; don't be anxious, because I am your God. I keep on strengthening you; I'm truly helping you. I'm surely upholding you with my victorious right hand."

<sup>{The Coming Defeat of God's Enemies}</sup> d "Look! All who are enraged at you will be put to shame and disgraced; those who contend with you will all die. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads will become nothing and die ; cf. LXX}</sup>
Those who quarrel with you <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX read You will seek them and not find those who quarrel with you}</sup> will be as nothing; those who fight you like nothing at all!"
<sup>{A Call to Courage}</sup> d "For I am the Lord your God, who takes hold of your right hand, who says to you, «Don't be afraid. I'll help you.

Don't be afraid, you little worm Jacob, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> you insects of Israel! I myself will help you,'<sup>{1QIsa a is masculine; MT is feminine}</sup> declares the Lord, your <sup>{1QIsa a is masculine; MT is feminine}</sup> Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel."

<sup>{A Promise of Victory}</sup> d "See, I'm making you <sup>{1QIsa a is masculine; MT is feminine}</sup> into a new, sharp, and multi-tooth threshing sledge. You'll thresh and crush the mountains, and make the hills like chaff.

You'll winnow them, and the wind will lift them up, and a tempest will blow them away. Then you'll rejoice in the Lord , and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> you'll make your boast in the Holy One of Israel."

"As for the poor, the needy, those seeking <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read poor and the needy seeking}</sup> water when there is none and their tongues are parched from thirst I, the Lord , will answer them. I, the God of Israel, won't abandon them.

I'll open up rivers on the barren heights, and fountains in the midst of the valleys. I'll turn the <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT implies the}</sup> desert into a pool of water, and the parched land into springs of water.
I'll put cedar trees in the wilderness, along with acacia, myrtle, and olive trees. I'll plant cypresses in the desert box <sup>{Or elm}</sup> trees, and pine trees together

all so that people may see and recognize, perceive, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa a corrector MT LXX read to consider}</sup> consider, and comprehend at the same time, that the hand of the Lord has done this, and that the Holy One of Israel has created it."
<sup>{Can God's Enemies Predict the Future?}</sup> d "Put forward your case!" says the Lord . "Submit your arguments!" says Jacob's King.

Let them approach and ask us, «What will happen? As to the former things, what were they? Tell us, so that we may consider them and know. Or <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads them. And we may know}</sup> the latter things or the things to come let us hear.

Tell us what the future holds, so we may know that you are gods! Yes, do something good or something bad, so we may hear <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads stare at one another ; cf. LXX}</sup> and gaze at it together.»"
"Look! You and your work are less than nothing; <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads you are nothing, and your work means nothing}</sup> whoever finds you pleasing is disgusting."

"You are stirring up <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read I am raising}</sup> one from the north, and they are coming <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads I am raising}</sup> from the rising of the sun; and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack and}</sup> he will be called by his <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read my}</sup> name. Rulers will arrive like mud; <sup>{I.e. like an overflowing river; so 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads He will come, rulers like mud}</sup> just <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads and}</sup> like a potter, he will trample the clay.

Who told of this from the beginning, so we could know, or beforehand, so we could ask, «Is it right? '<sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads He was right}</sup> Indeed, no one told of this, no one made an announcement, and no one heard your words:
First, to Zion: "There is slumber." <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads look, there they are}</sup> And to Jerusalem: "I'll send a messenger with good news."
But when I look, there is no one among them there's no one to give counsel, no one to give an answer when I ask them.
See, none of them exist, and their deeds are nothing. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads all of their works are utterly nothing}</sup> Their metal images are only wind and confusion.»"

<sup>{The Servant of the Lord}</sup> d "Here is my servant, whom I support, my chosen one, in whom I delight. <sup>{Lit. whom my soul delights}</sup> I've placed my Spirit upon him; and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack and}</sup> he'll deliver his <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack his}</sup> justice throughout the world. <sup>{Lit. justice to the nations}</sup>
He won't shout, or raise his voice, or make it heard in the street.

A crushed reed he will not break, and a fading candle <sup>{Lit. fading linen wick}</sup> he won't snuff out. <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads quench it}</sup> He'll bring forth <sup>{I.e. will demonstrate}</sup> justice for the truth.

And <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa h MT lack And}</sup> he won't grow faint or be crushed until he establishes justice on the mainland, and the coastlands take ownership of <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read the islands wait for (cf. 4QIsa h )}</sup> his Law."

<sup>{God Speaks about the Servant}</sup> d This is what God says the God <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa a corrector reads and ; MT reads the Lord}</sup> who created the heavens and stretched them out, who spread out the earth and its produce, who gives breath to the people on it and life <sup>{Lit. spirit}</sup> to those who walk in it:

"I've <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa h MT read I, the Lord, have ; LXX reads I, the Lord God, have}</sup> called you in righteousness. I'll take hold of your hand. I'll preserve you and appoint you as a covenant to the people, <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT LXX; 4QIsa h reads as an everlasting covenant}</sup> as a light for the nations,

to open blind eyes and to bring out those who are bound <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; 4QIsa h MT read out prisoners}</sup> from their cells, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa h MT lack and}</sup> those sitting in darkness from prison.
I, the Lord , am the one, and I won't give my name and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa h MT LXX read I am the LORD; that is my name. I won't give my}</sup> glory to another, nor my praise to idols.
See, the former things have taken place, and I'm announcing the <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa b 4QIsa h MT LXX lack the}</sup> new things before they spring into being I'm telling you about them."

<sup>{Praise in Song to God}</sup> d Sing to the Lord a new song, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa h MT lack and}</sup> his praise from the ends of the earth, you who sail down the sea and by everything in it, you coastlands and their inhabitants.

Let the desert cry out, <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa h LXX (singular); MT (plural)}</sup> its towns and the <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read and its towns}</sup> villages where Kedar lives; and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> let those who live in Sela sing for joy. Let them shout aloud <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads them cry joyfully}</sup> from the mountaintops.
Let them give glory to the Lord , and declare his praise in the islands.

The Lord marches out like a warrior; he stirs up his rage like a man of war; he makes his anger heard; he shouts aloud; <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads He makes a war cry and shouts out his anger}</sup> he declares his mastery over his enemies:

"I have certainly <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks certainly}</sup> stayed silent for a long time; I've kept still and held myself back. Now, like a woman giving birth, I'll cry out. All of a sudden I'll gasp and pant.
I'll devastate the mountains and hills, and dry up all their vegetation; I'll turn rivers into islands, and dry up the ponds.

I'll help the blind walk, even <sup>{Or and ; so 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks even}</sup> on a road they do not know; I'll guide them in directions <sup>{Lit. paths}</sup> they do not know. I'll turn the dark places <sup>{So 1QIsa a (misspelling places ); MT LXX read darkness}</sup> into light in front of them, and the rough places into level ground. These are the things I will do, and I won't abandon them.

Those who trust in carved idols will turn back and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> be completely disappointed, <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT LXX; MT ms lacks disappointed}</sup> along with those <sup>{1QIsa a MT LXX lack along with those}</sup> who say to metal images, «You are our gods.»"
<sup>{God Rebukes Israel}</sup> d "Listen, you deaf people, and look up, you blind people, so you may see!
Who is blind except my servant, or deaf like my messenger I am sending? Who is blind like the one committed to me, or blind like the Lord's servant?

You've seen <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; MT qere reads To see (or He sees )}</sup> many things, but you pay no <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT LXX; MT mss read he pays no}</sup> attention. His ears are open, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads to open ; or are open}</sup> but he doesn't listen.
The Lord was pleased, for the sake of his vindication, that he should magnify his Law and make it glorious.

But this is a people who have been robbed and plundered, all of them trapped in pits or hidden away in prisons. <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT LXX; 4QIsa g reads prison}</sup> They have become prey, with no one to rescue them; they have been made loot, with no one to say, «Send them back!'
"Who among you will listen, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack and}</sup> pay attention, and listen for the time to come?"

<sup>{God Punishes Israel}</sup> d "Who handed Jacob over to looters, and Israel to robbers? Was it not the Lord , against whom we have sinned? After all, they weren't willing to walk in his ways, and they wouldn't obey <sup>{Or wouldn't listen to}</sup> his instruction,

so he drenched him with <sup>{Lit. he poured out on him}</sup> the heat that is his anger, <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; 4QIsa g MT read the heat, his anger}</sup> the violence of war. It enveloped him in flames, but still he had no insight. It burned him up, but he didn't take it to heart."

<sup>{The Future Redemption of Israel}</sup> d But now this is what the Lord says, the one who created you, Jacob, the one who formed you, Israel: "Do not be afraid, because I've redeemed you. I've called you by name; you are mine.
When you pass through the waters, I'll be with you; and through the rivers, they won't sweep over you. when you walk through fire you won't be scorched, and the flame won't set you ablaze.

"I <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b 4QIsa g MT LXX read For I}</sup> am the Lord your God, the Holy One of Israel, your Redeemer . <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read Savior}</sup> And <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks And}</sup> I've given Egypt as your ransom, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX read as your ransom Egypt}</sup> Cush and the people of Seba <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b 4QIsa g MT read and Seba}</sup> in exchange for you.
Since you're precious in my sight and honored, and because I love you, I'm giving up people in your place, and nations in exchange for your life."
<sup>{The Regathering of Israel}</sup> d "Don't be afraid, for I am with you; I'll bring your children from the east, and gather you from the west.

I'll say to the north, «Give them up'! and to the south, «Don't keep them back!'Bring <sup>{1QIsa a employs masculine plural; MT employs feminine plural}</sup> my sons from far away and my daughters from the ends of the earth
everyone who is called by my name, whom I created for my glory, whom <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; 1QIsa b reads and whom}</sup> I formed and made.
"Bring out the people who are blind, yet still have eyes, who are deaf, yet still have ears!

Let all the nations be gathered together, and let the peoples be assembled. "Who is there among them who <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack who}</sup> can declare this, or announce <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads announce to us ; LXX reads announce to you}</sup> the former things? Let them produce their witnesses to prove them right, and let them proclaim <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads hear}</sup> so people will say, «It's true.'

"You are my witnesses,"declares the Lord , "and my servant whom I have chosen, so that you may know and trust me and understand that I am the One. <sup>{Or am he}</sup> Before me no God was formed, nor will there be one after me.
I, yes I, am the Lord , and apart from me there is no savior.
I've revealed and saved and proclaimed, when there was no foreign god among you and you are my witnesses," declares the Lord .

"I am God; also <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads God Yes, ; cf. LXX}</sup> from ancient days <sup>{Or from this day on,}</sup> I am the one. And there is no one who can deliver out of my hand; when I act, who can reverse it?"

This is what the Lord says, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: "For your sake I will send to Babylon, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa b MT each spell this line differently}</sup> and bring them all down as fugitives. Now as for the Babylonians, their ringing cry will become lamentation.
I am the Lord , your Holy One, Creator of Israel, and your King."
<sup>{Something New for Israel}</sup> d This is what the Lord says who makes a way through the sea, a path through the mighty waters,

who brings out chariots and horsemen, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; the Heb. lacks and}</sup> armies and warriors at the same time. They lay there, never to rise again, extinguished, snuffed out like a candle: <sup>{Lit. linen wick}</sup>
"Don't remember <sup>{1QIsa a employs second person singular; MT LXX employ second person plural}</sup> the former things; don't dwell on things past.

Watch! I'm about to carry out something new! And <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the Heb. lacks And}</sup> now it's springing up don't you recognize it? I'm making a way in the wilderness and paths <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read streams}</sup> in the desert.

Wild animals, jackals, and owls <sup>{Or ostriches}</sup> will honor me because I provide <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads have provided}</sup> water in the desert and streams in the wilderness to give drink to my people, my chosen ones, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads my chosen people}</sup>
the people whom I formed for myself and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa g lacks my chosen ones}</sup> so that they may speak <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa g MT LXX read recount}</sup> my praise."
<sup>{God is Weary of Israel}</sup> d "And <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; MT mss lack And}</sup> yet you didn't call upon me, Jacob; indeed, you are tired of me, Israel!

You haven't brought me your sheep for a burnt-offering, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads for your burnt-offerings ; or your burnt-offering}</sup> nor have you honored me with <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; implicit in MT}</sup> your sacrifices, nor have you made meal offerings for me <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa g MT read I have not burdened you with grain offerings ; LXX lacks this line}</sup> yet I have not tired you about incense!

You <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; 4QIsa g reads And you}</sup> haven't bought me sweet cane with money, nor have you satisfied me with the fat of your sacrifices. You have only burdened me with your sins and made me tired with your iniquities.

"I, I am the one who blots out your transgression <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads transgressions ; cf. LXX}</sup> for my own sake, and I'll remember your sins no more. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads not remember your sins}</sup>
Recount the brief! Let's argue the matter together; Present your case, so that you may be proved right.
Your first ancestor sinned, and your mediators rebelled against me.

So I'll disgrace the leaders of the Temple, and I'll consign Jacob to total destruction <sup>{The Heb. term refers to involuntary dedication to God of the thing destroyed}</sup> and Israel to contempt.
<sup>{God's Blessing on Israel}</sup> d "But now listen, Jacob my servant and Israel whom I have chosen:

This what the Lord says, the one who made you, formed you from the womb, and who will help <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads he will help}</sup> you: "Don't be afraid, Jacob my servant, and Jeshurun, <sup>{I.e. a poetic term for national Israel; the Heb. name means Upright One ; cf. Deut 32:15; 33:5, 26}</sup> whom I have chosen.
For I'll pour water upon thirsty ground and streams on parched land. So <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT lacks So}</sup> will I pour my Spirit upon your offspring, and my blessing upon your descendants.
They'll <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read And they}</sup> spring up as among <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT mss LXX Targ; MT reads up among}</sup> the green grass, like willows by flowing streams.
One will say, «I belong to the Lord ,'and another will call himself by the name of Jacob; still another will have written on his hand, «the Lord's,'and will adopt the name of Israel."

<sup>{I am the First and the Last}</sup> d This is what the Lord says, the King of Israel and its Redeemer the Lord of the Heavenly Armies is his name <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack is his name}</sup> "I am the first and I am the last, and apart from me there is no God.

Who is like me? Let him proclaim and declare it, and lay it out for himself <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read me}</sup> since he made <sup>{Or himself, making him ; so 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read since I made}</sup> an ancient people. And let him speak <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa c MT LXX lack let him speak}</sup> future events; let them tell him what <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa c MT read and what}</sup> will happen.

Don't tremble, and don't be afraid. <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; LXX lacks and do not be afraid}</sup> Didn't I tell you and announce it long ago? You are my witnesses. Is there any God besides me? There is no other Rock I don't know of any."

<sup>{A Rebuke to Idol Worship}</sup> d Now, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack Now}</sup> all the forming of <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read those who form}</sup> images means nothing, and the things they treasure are worthless. Their own witnesses cannot see, and they <sup>{So MT LXX; 1QIsa a reads They}</sup> know nothing. So they will be put to shame.
Who would shape a god or cast an image that profits nothing?

To be sure, all who associate with it will be put to shame; and as for the craftsmen, they are only human. Let them all gather together and <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT lacks and}</sup> take their stand. Then <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack Then}</sup> let them be terrified they will be humiliated together.

The blacksmith prepares a tool and works in the coals, then <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack then}</sup> fashions an idol with hammers, working by the strength of his arm. He even becomes hungry and loses his strength; he drinks no water and grows faint.

The carpenter measures it <sup>{I.e. the idol; so 1QIsa a LXX; MT lacks it}</sup> with a line; he traces its shape with a stylus, then fashions it with planes and shapes it with a compass. He makes the idol like a human figure, with human beauty, to be at home <sup>{Lit. to rest}</sup> in a shrine.
He cuts down cedars, or chooses a cypress tree or an oak, and lets it grow strong among the trees of the forest. Or he plants a cedar, and the rain makes it grow.

He divides it up <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads It is ; LXX reads so that it is}</sup> for people to burn. Taking part of it, he warms himself, makes a fire, and bakes bread. Or perhaps <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads Also}</sup> he constructs a god and worships it. He makes it an idol and bows down to it.

Half the wood he burns in the fire, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT lacks and}</sup> over <sup>{1QIsa a lacks over , but inserts it above the line}</sup> that half he places <sup>{Lit. half is}</sup> meat so he can eat. He sits by its coals, warms himself, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read eats meat he roasted as a roast and is satisfied. He also warms himself}</sup> and says, "Ah! I am warm in front of <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads I see ; LXX reads and I have seen}</sup> the fire."

And the rest of it he makes into a god. To blocks <sup>{Or to his Baals (i.e. to Canaanite deities); so 1QIsa a copyist error; MT LXX read god, for his idol}</sup> of wood he bows down, worships, prays, and says, "Save me, since you are my god."
They don't realize; they don't understand, because their eyes are plastered over so they cannot see, and their minds, too, so they cannot understand.

No one stops to think. No one has the knowledge or understanding to think yes to think! <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT lacks yes to think!}</sup> "Half of it I burned in the fire.
