His <sup>{I.e. Israel's; so 1QIsa a MT q}</sup> watchmen <sup>{MT reads His watchman ; LXX reads Look! They all}</sup> are blind; they are all without knowledge. They are all dumb dogs they cannot bark. They keep on dreaming and lying around, and they're lovers of sleep! <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads sleeping}</sup>

Meanwhile, <sup>{Lit. And}</sup> the dogs have a mighty appetite they can never get enough. And as for them, they are the shepherds <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads shepherds ; LXX reads evil}</sup> who lack understanding; they have all turned to their own way, each one to his gain, each and every one.

«Come!'they say, «let's <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT ms ; 1QIsa b reads I will ; MT reads let me}</sup> have some wine, and let's fill ourselves with strong drink! Then, <sup>{Or And}</sup> tomorrow will be like today, <sup>{1QIsa a reads this the day ; 1QIsa b MT read this day}</sup> or even much better!»"

<sup>{Israel's Idolatry}</sup> d "Also <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT lacks Also ; cf. LXX}</sup> the righteous are perishing, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX read righteous person has perished}</sup> but no one takes it to heart; devout people <sup>{Lit. people of the mercy ; so 1QIsa a MT reads people of mercy ; LXX reads just men}</sup> are taken away, while no one understands that the righteous person is taken away from calamity.

Then <sup>{Or and ; so 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack Then}</sup> he enters into peace, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT lacks and}</sup> they'll rest on his <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT read their}</sup> couches, each one living righteously. <sup>{Lit. one walking in his uprightness ; so 1QIsa a 1QIsa b ; MT reads her uprightness}</sup>
"But as for you, come here, you children of a sorceress, you offspring of adulterers and prostitutes! <sup>{So LXX (cf. Syriac); 1QIsa a MT read she has practiced prostitution}</sup>

Whom are you mocking? And <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; 1QIsa b MT lack And}</sup> against whom do you make a wide mouth and stick out your tongue? Are you not children of transgression, the offspring of lies,
you who burn with lust among the oaks, under every spreading tree, who slaughter your children in the ravines, under the clefts of the rocks?

"Among the smooth stones <sup>{I.e. among the idols}</sup> of the ravines is your portion there they are as <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa i MT reads they yes, they! are ; LXX reads there this is}</sup> your lot. To them you have poured out drink offerings; you have brought grain offerings. Should I be lenient over such things?
"You have made your bed on a high and lofty mountain, and you went up to offer sacrifice there.

Behind the doors and the doorposts you have set up your pagan sign." For in deserting me you have uncovered your bed you have climbed up into it and have opened it wide. And you <sup>{1QIsa a reads sing.; MT reads pl.}</sup> have made a pact for yourself with them; you have loved their bed, you have looked on their private parts. <sup>{Lit. their hand ; i.e. a euphemism for the male sex organ}</sup>

You went to Molech <sup>{I.e. to the Canaanite deity; or to the king}</sup> with olive oil and increased your perfumes; you sent your ambassadors far away, you sent them down even to Sheol <sup>{I.e. the afterlife}</sup> itself!

You grew tired with your many wanderings, <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads your wandering}</sup> but you wouldn't say: «It is hopeless.'You found new strength for your desire, and so you did not falter.

"Whom did you so dread and while you feared me <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa d MT LXX read and fear}</sup> that you lied, and you did not remember me, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa d LXX; MT lacks and}</sup> did not lay to heart these things? <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa d MT lack thing ; LXX reads lay me to heart}</sup> Haven't I remained silent for a long time, and still you don't fear me?"

"I will denounce your righteousness <sup>{1QIsa a MT; 4QIsa d reads justice}</sup> and your works, for your collections of idols <sup>{1QIsa a lacks of idols ; 4QIsa d MT LXX read for they}</sup> will not benefit you.

When you cry out, let your collection deliver you! The wind will carry them all off, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT lacks and}</sup> a mere breath will sweep them all away." <sup>{God's Reward for the Faithful}</sup> d"But whoever <sup>{Lit. But the one ; so 4QIsa d MT; 1QIsa a reads But one (i.e. without article)}</sup> takes refuge in me will possess the land, and will inherit my holy mountain.

And one has said: <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads one will say ; or I will say ; LXX reads they will say}</sup> «Build up! Build up the road! <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT lacks the road}</sup> Prepare the highway! Remove every obstacle from my people's way.'

"For this is what the high and lofty One says, who inhabits eternity, whose name is Holy: "He lives <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa d ; MT reads I live}</sup> in the height and in holiness, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa d MT read in the high and holy place}</sup> and also with the one who is of a contrite and humble spirit, to revive the spirit of the humble, and to revive the heart of the contrite.
For I won't accuse forever, nor will I always be angry; for then the human spirit would grow faint before me even the souls that I have created.

Because of his wicked greed I was angry, so I punished him; and <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa d LXX; 1QIsa b MT lack and}</sup> I hid my face, and was angry but he kept turning back to his stubborn will. <sup>{Lit. into the way of his heart}</sup>

I've seen his ways, <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT LXX; 4QIsa d reads way}</sup> yet I will heal him, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads him, and I will guide him ; LXX reads him, and I will exhort him}</sup> and restore for him <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; the reading is probably an error: MT LXX lack for him}</sup> comfort <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT use different but related words}</sup> to him and for those who mourn for him <sup>{Lit. for his mourners}</sup>

when <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b 4QIsa d MT lack when}</sup> I create the fruit of the lips: Peace <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT read Peace, peace cf. LXX}</sup> to the one who is far away or near," says the Lord , "and I'll heal him.

But the wicked are tossed like the sea; <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; 4QIsa d MT read are like the tossing sea}</sup> for it is not able to <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 4QIsa d MT lack able to}</sup> keep still, and its waters toss up mire and mud.
"Yet <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack Yet}</sup> there is no peace,"says my God, "for the wicked."

<sup>{False and True Worship}</sup> d "Shout aloud! Don't hold back! Lift up your voice like a trumpet! Declare to my people their rebellions, <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; 1QIsa b MT read rebellion}</sup> and to the house of Jacob their sins.

They <sup>{So 1QIsa a 1QIsa b 4QIsa d LXX; MT reads And they}</sup> seek me day after day, <sup>{Lit. me day and day ; so 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b 4QIsa d MT read me day, day}</sup> and are eager to know my ways, as if they were a nation that practices righteousness and has not forsaken the justice of their God. "They ask me to reveal just decisions; they are eager to draw near to God.

«Why have we fasted,'they ask, <sup>{1QIsa a 1QIsa b 4QIsa d LXX MT lack they ask}</sup> «but you do not see? «Why have we humbled ourselves,'<sup>{So 1QIsa a 1QIsa b LXX; MT reads ourself}</sup> they ask, <sup>{1QIsa a 1QIsa b 4QIsa d LXX MT lack they ask}</sup> «but you take no notice?»" <sup>{Fasting that God Approves}</sup> d"Look! On your fast day you serve your own interest and oppress all your workers.

"Look! You fast only for quarreling, and for <sup>{So 1QIsa b 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack for}</sup> fighting, and for hitting with wicked fists. You cannot fast as you do today and have your voice heard on high.

"Is this the kind of fast that I have chosen, merely a day for a person to humble himself? Is it merely for bowing down one's head like a bulrush, for lying <sup>{So 1QIsa a 1QIsa b ; MT LXX read and for lying}</sup> on sackcloth and ashes? Is this what you <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa d LXX (plural); 1QIsa b MT (sing.)}</sup> call a fast, an <sup>{So 1QIsa a 1QIsa b ; MT reads and an}</sup> acceptable day to the Lord ?

Isn't this the <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX lack the}</sup> fast that <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX lack that}</sup> I have been choosing: to loose the bonds of injustice, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX lack and}</sup> to untie the cords of the yoke, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a MT; 1QIsa b 4QIsa d LXX lack and}</sup> to let the oppressed go free, and to break every yoke?

Isn't it to share your bread with the hungry, and to bring the homeless poor into your house; when you see the naked, to cover him with clothing, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX lack with clothing}</sup> and not to raise yourself up <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT read to hide yourself ; LXX reads to disregard}</sup> from your own flesh and blood?"

<sup>{God's Reward}</sup> d "Then your light will break forth like the dawn, and your healing will spring up quickly; and your vindication will go before you, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a 1QIsa b LXX; MT lacks and}</sup> the glory of the lord will guard your back.

Then you'll call, and the Lord will answer; you'll cry for help, and he'll respond, «Here I am. '"If you do away with the yoke among you, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; 1QIsa b MT lack and}</sup> pointing fingers and malicious talk;
if you pour yourself out for the hungry and satisfy the needs of afflicted souls, then your light will rise in darkness, and your night will be like noonday.

And the Lord will guide you continuously, and satisfy your soul in parched places, <sup>{1QIsa a spells the word places incorrectly}</sup> and they <sup>{So 1QIsa a 1QIsa b ; MT reads he ; LXX reads and your bones will be strengthened}</sup> will strengthen your bones; and you'll be like a watered garden, like a spring of water, whose waters never fail.

And your people will rebuild the ancient ruins; You'll raise up the age-old foundations, <sup>{Lit. the foundations of many generations}</sup> and people will call you <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX read you will be called}</sup> «Repairer of Broken Walls,'«Restorer of Streets to Live In.'

"If you keep your feet from trampling the Sabbath, from <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa n ; 1QIsa b MT lack from}</sup> pursuing your own interests on my holy day, if you call the Sabbath a delight and <sup>{So 1QIsa a 1QIsa b 4QIsa n ; MT LXX lack and}</sup> the Lord's holy day honorable; and if you honor it by not going your own ways <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa n MT; 1QIsa b reads way}</sup> and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT lack and}</sup> seeking your own pleasure or speaking merely idle <sup>{1QIsa a 1QIsa b 4QIsa n MT LXX lack merely idle}</sup> words,

then you will take delight in the Lord , and he <sup>{So 1QIsa a 1QIsa b 4QIsa n LXX; MT reads and I}</sup> will make you ride upon the heights of the earth; and he <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; 1QIsa b 4QIsa n MT read and I}</sup> will make you feast on the inheritance of your ancestor Jacob, your father. "Yes! The mouth of the Lord has spoken."
<sup>{Sins that Separate from God}</sup> d "See, the Lord's hand is not too short to save, nor are his ears <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read ear}</sup> too dull to hear.
Instead, your iniquities have been barriers between you and your God, and your sins have concealed his face from you so that he won't listen.
For your hands are defiled with blood, and your fingers with iniquity; your tongue <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read your lips have spoken lies, your tongue}</sup> mutters wickedness.

No one brings a lawsuit fairly, and no one goes to law honestly; they have relied <sup>{So 1QIsa a 1QIsa b ; MT reads they rely}</sup> on empty arguments and they tell lies; they conceive <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT reads to conceive}</sup> trouble and give birth <sup>{So 1QIsa a 1QIsa b ; MT reads and to give birth}</sup> to iniquity.

They hatch <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT reads They have hatched ; LXX reads hatched}</sup> adders'eggs <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; 1QIsa b MT read an adder's eggs}</sup> and weave <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads weave , but with a different Heb. word}</sup> a spider's web; whoever eats their eggs dies, and any crushed egg hatches out futility. <sup>{Lit. a viper ; so 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX utilize feminine form}</sup>
Their cobwebs cannot become clothing, they cannot cover themselves with what they make. Their deeds are deeds of iniquity, and acts of violence fill their hands.

Their feet rush to evil, and they are quick to shed innocent blood. Their thoughts are thoughts of iniquity; ruin, destruction, and violence <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack and violence}</sup> are in their paths.
The pathway of peace they do not know, and there is no justice in their courses. They have made their roads crooked; no one who walks in them will know peace."

<sup>{A Commitment to Wait on God}</sup> d "So justice is far from us, and righteousness does not reach us. We wait for light, but look there is darkness; we wait for brightness, but we walk in deep darkness. <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads darknesses}</sup>

Let's grope <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads We grope LXX reads They grope}</sup> along the wall like the blind; let us grope like those who have no eyes. We stumble at midday as if it were twilight, in desolate places <sup>{Or among vigorous people}</sup> like dead people.

We all growl like bears; we <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads and we}</sup> sigh mournfully like doves. We look for justice, but there is none, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; Not in MT LXX}</sup> for deliverance, but it's far from us.

"For our transgressions before you are many, and our sins testify <sup>{1QIsa a cf. LXX; MT reads sin testifies}</sup> against us; for our transgressions are with us, and as for our iniquities, we acknowledge them:

they've rebelled <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads rebellion LXX reads we have sinned}</sup> in <sup>{Lit. and}</sup> treachery against the Lord , and are turning away from following our God; and they've spoken <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads speaking LXX reads we have spoken}</sup> oppression and revolt, and are conceiving <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads conceiving and uttering ; LXX reads we have conceived and thought about}</sup> lying words from the heart.

I'll drive back justice, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads Justice is driven back LXX reads We withdrew justice}</sup> and righteousness stands at a distance; for truth has fallen in the public square, and honesty cannot enter.
Truth is missing, and whoever turns away from evil becomes a prey." <sup>{God Brings His Own Salvation}</sup> d"Then the Lord looked, and it displeased him that there was no justice.
He saw that there was no one, and was appalled that there was no one to intervene; so his own arm <sup>{I.e. the Messiah}</sup> brought him victory, and his righteous acts upheld him.
He put on righteousness like a breastplate, and a helmet of salvation on his head; he put on garments of vengeance for clothing, and wrapped himself in fury like a cloak.
So he will repay according to their action: Anger to his enemies, retribution to his foes; to the coastlands he will render their due.

So people will fear the name of the Lord from the west, and his glories <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads glory LXX reads his glorious name}</sup> from the rising of the sun; for he will come as a pent-up stream that the breath of the Lord drives along.
"And a Redeemer will come to Zion, to those in Jacob who turn from transgression,"says the Lord .

"As for me, this is my covenant with them,"<sup>{So 1QIsa a MT mss LXX; 1QIsa b MT read them , but the reading contains a grammatical object error}</sup> says the Lord . "And <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX lack And}</sup> my Spirit that is upon you, and my words that I have put in your mouth, won't depart from your mouth, or from the mouths of your children, or from the mouths of your children's children, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX read children, says the Lord,}</sup> from now on and forever."
<sup>{The Light of God's Deliverance}</sup> d "Arise, shine! For your light has come; the <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX read and the glory}</sup> glory of the Lord has risen upon you.
For look! Darkness will cover the earth and thick darkness is over the people, <sup>{Lit. peoples}</sup> but the Lord will arise upon you, and his glory will appear over you.
Nations will come to your light, and kings before <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads kings to the brightness of ; cf. LXX}</sup> your dawn.
"Lift up your eyes and look around: They all gather together, they come to you; your sons will come from far away, and your daughters will be carried on the hip." <sup>{Or arm}</sup>

Then you will look and be radiant; your heart will swell with joy, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT read will throb and swell with joy LXX reads you will be amazed in your heart}</sup> because the abundance of the seas will be diverted to you, and the riches of the nations will come to you.

Throngs of camels will blanket you: the young camels of Midian and Ephu; <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT read Ephah}</sup> all those from Shebu <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT read Sheba}</sup> will come. They'll carry gold and frankincense, and proclaim the praise of the Lord .

All Kedar's flocks will be gathered to you, the rams of Nebaioth will serve you. and <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; 1QIsa b MT lack and}</sup> they'll come up with acceptance upon <sup>{So 1QIsa a cf. LXX; 1QIsa b MT read upon the acceptance of}</sup> my altar, and I'll glorify my glorious house."
<sup>{The Future Restoration of Zion}</sup> d "Who are these that fly like clouds, and like doves to their windows? <sup>{I.e. dovecotes , cages in which pet doves are housed}</sup>

For the coastlands will look to me, with the ships of Tarshish in the lead, to bring my <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX read your}</sup> children from far away, their silver and gold with them, to the name of the Lord your God, and to the Holy One of Israel, because he has glorified you.
"Foreigners will rebuild your walls, and their kings will serve you. Though in my anger I struck you down, in my favor I have shown you mercy.

Your gates will always stand open by day or night, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX lack and}</sup> they will not be shut, so that nations will bring you their wealth, with their kings led in procession.
For the nation or kingdom that will not serve you will perish; those nations will be utterly ruined.

"He has given you <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX lack He has given you}</sup> the glory of Lebanon, and it will come <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX read Lebanon will come}</sup> to you, the cypress, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT lack and}</sup> the plane tree, <sup>{i.e. a species of trees that could readily be stripped of their bark; cf. Gen 30:37}</sup> and the pine, to adorn the place of my sanctuary; and I will make the place of my feet glorious.

"All <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX lack All}</sup> the descendants of those who oppressed you will come bending low before you, and all those who despised you will bow down at your feet. They'll call you «The City of the Lord ,'«Zion of the Holy One of Israel.»"
<sup>{Israel: the Joy of Generations}</sup> d "Although you have been forsaken and despised, with no one traveling through, I will make you the everlasting pride, the joy of all generations.
You'll suck the milk of nations, You'll suck the breasts of kings. Then you will realize that I, the Lord , am your Savior and your Redeemer, the Mighty One of Jacob.

"Instead of bronze, I'll bring gold, and instead of iron, I'll bring silver; instead of wood, bronze, and instead of stones, iron. I'll appoint peace as your supervisor and righteousness as your taskmaster.

Then <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; 1QIsa b MT lack Then}</sup> violence will no longer be heard in your land, nor devastation or destruction within your borders; but you'll call your walls «Salvation', and your gates «Praise'.

"The sun will no longer be your light by day, nor for brightness will the moon shine on you by night <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX. Not in 1QIsa b MT}</sup> for the Lord will be your everlasting light, <sup>{1QIsa b lacks and your God light ; 1QIsa a MT LXX contain the longer reading}</sup> and your God will be your glory.
Your sun won't <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT reads will no longer}</sup> set, nor will your moon withdraw itself for the Lord will be your everlasting light, and your days of mourning will end.

Then your people will all be righteous; They'll possess the land forever. They are the shoot <sup>{So 1QIsa a 4QIsa m MT; 1QIsa a MT ms lacks the shoot ; LXX reads Guarding}</sup> that the Lord planted, <sup>{Lit. of the plantings of the Lord ; so 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b reads of his plantings MT reads of his planting MT qere reads of my planting LXX reads the planting}</sup> the works <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT read work}</sup> of his hands, so that I might be glorified.
The least of them will become a thousand, and the smallest one a mighty nation. "I am the Lord ; When the time is right, <sup>{Lit. In its time}</sup> I will do this swiftly."

<sup>{Good News of Deliveerance}</sup> d "The Spirit of the Lord <sup>{So 1QIsa a 1QIsa b LXX; 4QIsa m MT read the Lord God}</sup> is upon me, because the Lord has anointed me; he has sent me to bring good news to the oppressed and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lacks and}</sup> to bind up the brokenhearted, to proclaim freedom for the captives, and release from darkness <sup>{Or prison ; or and opening of the eyes}</sup> for the prisoners;
to proclaim the year of the Lord's favor, the <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX ms ; 4QIsa b MT LXX read and the}</sup> day of vengeance of our God; to comfort all who mourn;

to provide for those who grieve in Zion to bestow on them a crown of beauty instead of ashes, the oil of gladness instead of mourning, a mantle of praise instead of a spirit of despair." "Then people will call them <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read they will be called}</sup> "Oaks of Righteousness" , "The Planting of the Lord" , in order to display his splendor.

They will rebuild the ancient ruins; they will restore the places long devastated; they will build again the ruined cities, they will build again <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lacks again}</sup> the places devastated for many generations.
Strangers will stand and feed your flocks, and foreigners will work your land and dress your vines.

But as for you, you will be called priests of the Lord , and <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lacks and}</sup> you will be named ministers of our God. You will feed on the wealth of the nations, and you will boast about their riches.

Instead of your shame you will receive double, and instead of disgrace people will shout with joy over your <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads their}</sup> inheritance; therefore you <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX reads they}</sup> will inherit a double portion in their land; everlasting joy will be yours." <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads theirs ; LXX reads over their head}</sup>

"For I, the Lord, love justice, and <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT lacks and}</sup> I hate robbery and iniquity; I will faithfully present your reward <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LX read present their reward}</sup> and make an everlasting covenant with you. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read with them}</sup>

Your <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read Their}</sup> offspring will be known among the nations, and your <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX read their}</sup> descendants among the people. <sup>{Lit. peoples}</sup> All who see them will acknowledge them, that they are an offspring the Lord has blessed."

<sup>{Rejoicing in God's Deliverance}</sup> d "I will heartily rejoice in the Lord , my soul will delight in my God; for he has wrapped me in garments of salvation; he has arrayed me in a robe of righteousness, just like a bridegroom, like a priest <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads bridegroom decks himself like a priest ; LXX reads bridegroom decks me}</sup> with a garland, and like a bride adorns herself with her jewels.

For just as the soil brings forth its shoots, and as a garden makes what is sown within it spring up, so the Lord God <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads the Lord G od ; LXX reads the Lord}</sup> will make righteousness and praise spring up before all the nations for Zion's sake." <sup>{So MT LXX; 1QIsa a ends v. 11 with for Zion's sake}</sup>

<sup>{The Vindication of Jerusalem}</sup> d "And <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT LXX lack And}</sup> I won't remain silent, <sup>{1QIsa a and MT use different Hebrew verbs for silent}</sup> and for Jerusalem's sake I won't stay quiet, until her vindication shines out like brightness, and her salvation like a burning torch.

The nations will see your vindication, and all the kings your glory; and people will call you <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; MT reads and you will be called ; LXX reads he will call you}</sup> by a new name that the mouth of the Lord will bestow.
You will be a crown of splendor in the Lord's hand, and a royal diadem in the hand of your God.

And <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; MT lacks And}</sup> you'll no longer be called «Deserted,'and your land will no longer be called «Desolate'; but people will call you <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT lacks will call you}</sup> «Hephzibah,'<sup>{The Heb. word Hephzibah means My Delight is in Her}</sup> and your land «Beulah'<sup>{The Heb. word Beulah means Married}</sup> for the Lord will take delight in you, and your land will be married."

"For just as <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; 1QIsa b MT lack For just as}</sup> a young man marries a maiden, so your sons will marry you; and just as a bridegroom rejoices over his bride, so your God will rejoice over you.

"Upon your walls, Jerusalem, I have posted watchmen; all day and all night they won't <sup>{So 1QIsa a 1QIsa b ; MT LXX read never}</sup> remain silent. You who make mention of the Lord , take no rest,
and give him no rest until he prepares, establishes <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX read he establishes}</sup> and makes Jerusalem a song of praise throughout the earth.

"The Lord has sworn by his right hand and by his mighty arm: <sup>{I.e. the Messiah}</sup> «I will never again give your grain <sup>{So 1QIsa a 1QIsa b ; MT reads I will never give your grain again}</sup> as food for your enemies; never <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX read and never}</sup> again will foreigners drink your new wine for which you have toiled;

but surely <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; 1QIsa b MT lack but surely}</sup> those who harvest it will eat it and praise the name of <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX lack the name of}</sup> the Lord , and those who gather it will drink it in the courts of my sanctuary,'says your God." <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX lack says your God}</sup>

<sup>{The Coming of God to Reign}</sup> d "Pass through <sup>{So 1QIsa a LXX; 1QIsa b MT read Pass through! Pass through}</sup> the gates! prepare the way for the people! Build up! Build up the highway! Clear it of stumbling stones, <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; cf. Isa 8:14; 1QIsa b MT LXX read of stones}</sup> speak among the peoples. <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX read raise a banner over the peoples}</sup>

Here is the Lord ! Proclaim <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX read See, the Lord has proclaimed}</sup> to the ends <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT LXX read end}</sup> of the earth, say to the inhabitants <sup>{Lit. daughter}</sup> of Zion: «See, your salvation is coming! See, his reward is with him, and his recompenses are <sup>{So 1QIsa a ; 1QIsa b MT read recompense is ; 1QIsa b MT LXX read work is}</sup> before him.'

<sup>{God's Day of Vengeance}</sup> d "Who is this coming from Edom, from Bozrah, in garments stained crimson? Who is this, robed in such splendor, marching in his great might? It is I, speaking in vindication, mighty to save.
